programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former marine falls to his death from a hotel balcony, and investigations quickly lead to the discovery of another body. As Gibbs and the team dig deeper, they uncover evidence that points to the activities of a vice ring trafficking young girls. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee tries to help his sister, who believes she may have murdered someone - but is too traumatised and disorientated to remember exactly what happened. As the team investigates the connected case of a sailor's death, Tony and Abby find themselves distracted by relationship problems. Drama, starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Two FBI agents try to seize control of the team's investigation into the discovery of a perfectly preserved body hidden in a chimney at a marine base. Reluctant to be ousted from the case, Gibbs agrees to work with them - and learns the victim was a serial killer responsible for the deaths of 14 people. Meanwhile, McGee's literary efforts lead to discord with the others

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a new fantasy game sweeping New York, and discover that harmless role-playing has begun to overlap with reality in deadly fashion. Meanwhile, a woman's death is traced to an exotic cuisine event where the dishes include live centipedes, Brazilian cockroaches, fried tarantulas and other dubious delicacies

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A woman's body is found in the water tank of a building in which six suspicious deaths have occurred over a 10-year period, making Lindsay wonder if the place is cursed. Meanwhile, Stella and Hawkes discover a man's corpse on a playground swing

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A corpse is discovered in a natural history museum, but the CSI detectives are unable to make an immediate identification because a swarm of flesh-eating beetles has partially devoured it. A six-year-old is later found hiding in the building, and proves to be a key witness. Meanwhile, Flack is called to a building site where a man has been poisoned with insulating foam

programme.desc: Dorien has a life-changing experience with a psychic, while Tracey takes charge of a boys' football team. Starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon, Tracey and Dorien manage to convince themselves Princess Diana has bought a local mansion, and enthusiastically prepare for the arrival of royal neighbours - will their attempts to raise Chigwell's social standing end in tears? Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina tries to give Peter the brush-off and Lewis agrees to speak up for his girlfriend's brother Rudy when he's pulled in for a minor offence. CID are brought in when witnesses involved in a serious assault case prove to have form for armed robbery

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Frustrated by his unrequited feelings for Faye, Linden takes out his anger on his new protege Oliver. Martha invites an old friend of Elliot to a surprise birthday party she is organising, and Penny tries to prove she is a better doctor than her brother - but both find they have a lot to learn. Meera Syal joins the cast

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nikki's slow recovery from the fall is set back by the devastating news that she can no longer work as a paramedic, while Abs tells Simon his irrational behaviour is beginning to affect Lara. Luke reluctantly attends the inquest, where Kate insists Greg's death was his fault, and after the verdict is announced, Luke makes the hasty decision to quit. Charlie also makes a decision about his future

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart plays a practical joke on Phil that ruins his investigation into a robbery at a nail bar. When Jack comes across the pair fighting in CID he jumps to the conclusion Phil is the guilty party and would learn more respect for the job by having a spell back on the beat in uniform. Roger is not impressed when Lewis ropes him into laborious paperwork to get some background on the mysterious skull Tash's brother Rudy discovered a few weeks ago

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy's back with a vengeance and Ricky returns from Paris to find a nasty shock awaits him. Mark decides to do some private investigation, while Pauline fears Arthur is losing his grip

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roy is in a festive mood while Pat is haunted by Christmas Past. Gita gets ready for her hot date and Mark is on a dangerous mission for justice with Ruth firmly by his side

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: After the family conference at which George has revealed his wishes, he immediately drives to Sarah to inform her of his long-term plans to divorce Regina and marry her. Elizabeth has a brain-wave regarding how to ruin Regina's reputation but she needs Sarah and Anna's help

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Claire is accused of stealing from a patient, and finds support from her colleagues in short supply, while Alex considers the possibility that Tom only wants her as a mother figure for baby Joe. Will also has a lot on his mind, not helped by the worrying case of a modern-day Noah who, although seemingly content with his lot, is showing signs of mental illness. Rural medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd and Eva Pope

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen is finally able to get up and about - and see the chaos that awaits her after Tristan's party the night before. James puts in an offer for a family home. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Lynda Bellingham and Peter Davison

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean is looking forward to the pitter-patter of tiny feet - despite the fact that daughter Judith is not even pregnant

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Social climber Hyacinth finds herself all at sea when she makes an unexpected discovery during a QE2 cruise that threatens to jeopardise her dream trip. Christmas special from 1993, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly spots a bottle with a message inside floating down the river, and decides to catch it - so enlists the help of Clegg, Billy and Alvin to follow its elusive course and eventually read the mysterious contents. Long-running comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Peter Sallis, Keith Clifford and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: Maisie rescues Gavin McKay, a man who borrowed £2,000 from a pair of villains and has ended up doing their dirty work - running an illegal drinking club and hiding a dead body. Pauline Quirke, Darren Tighe, Ian McElhinney and Steve John Shepherd star

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Etta Rose and Boyzey are Trudy's old school friends, and Etta is running for Alderman. However, her campaign office is a fire-bombed, prompting Boyzey to seek those responsible. Boyzey soon lands in trouble and the team works together to help him. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A Home Office pathologist joins the team to help discover the identity of a dead girl found in woodland in 1987. The detectives trace her origins back to the Highlands of Scotland, uncovering evidence of syphilis and a possible connection with local aristocrat Lady Deeley. Meanwhile, Lane confronts his fears by revisiting the crime scene. Detective drama, guest starring Timothy West and Jane Asher

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An MI5 informant in a major bank, who could allow an almost bankrupt British government access to £1billion of illegal earnings, refuses to share vital data with Section D. Ros is dispatched to bring him in, but assassins are also on his trail - and she gets caught in the crossfire as they try to silence him. Espionage drama, guest starring Ewen Bremner, with Hermione Norris

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart plays a practical joke on Phil that ruins his investigation into a robbery at a nail bar. When Jack comes across the pair fighting in CID he jumps to the conclusion Phil is the guilty party and would learn more respect for the job by having a spell back on the beat in uniform. Roger is not impressed when Lewis ropes him into laborious paperwork to get some background on the mysterious skull Tash's brother Rudy discovered a few weeks ago

programme.desc: A doctor is accused of murdering a seriously ill patient with a morphine overdose, a case which echoes a dilemma in Deed's personal life and sets him on the path to potential ruin. Meanwhile, Charlie's HIV-positive friend finds herself in danger of losing her baby to Social Services, and calls on the judge for help. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.desc: The 12th-century sleuth sets out to clear his novice Oswin of murdering a nun whose body has been found frozen in a block of ice. While tracking down the culprit, he is also forced to confront the fact he fathered a son before he took holy orders. Medieval mystery based on the novels of Ellis Peters, starring Derek Jacobi, Mark Charnock, Eoin McCarthy and Julian Firth

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: When the medieval monk investigates the murder of a wealthy cleric, the finger of suspicion immediately points to a new arrival at the abbey, but as Cadfael digs deeper, he uncovers connections to a family with tangled loyalties. Drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Ian McNeice, Christien Anholt and Julian Glover

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The monks cause a storm of resentment by launching an expedition to a Welsh village to recover the bones of St Winifred and return them to the abbey. As if that does not make them unpopular enough, a local landowner dies in mysterious circumstances and it is up to Brother Cadfael to clear his colleagues of murder and ensure they get out of the country alive. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Michael Culver, John Hallam and Anna Friel

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe and the faithful Harper begin their latest tour of duty by uncovering top-level corruption in the Army. Reporting their findings leads to attempts on their lives, so they fake their own deaths and re-enlist under new identities in an attempt to expose the culprits. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: After three months back in 2010, Alex is desperate to return to the 1980s, where Gene is on the run accused of trying to kill her, and the team is in disarray with Ray promoted to DI and a child's kidnapping to investigate. As the past merges into the present, she is visited by the ghost of a dead policeman and realises there are still dark secrets to unearth. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A serial killer targets women signed up to a dating agency, so Alex puts aside her examination of Sam Tyler's death back in Manchester to go undercover. However, as it becomes clear that Shaz matches the profile of previous victims, she steps forward to snare the murderer - an act set to be her last as a police officer. Fantasy crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Monserrat Lombard, with Beth Goddard

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Feature-length festive episode from 1992. A violent attack on an elderly Czech fighter pilot leads Lovejoy and Eric to hunt for missing diamonds in Prague, and the pair are soon joined by Jane, who was holidaying in Austria with friends. Meanwhile, Tinker is left to make preparations for Christmas. Guest starring Donald Pleasence, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: As the Brokenwood Golf Club gathers for its Wednesday morning round, a zombie-like figure lurches from the morning mist identified as Alison Stone, the wealthy club owner. Her face red and blistering, she collapses into a bunker and dies leaving Mike and Kristin to investigate an apparent homicide by poisoning. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Australian crime drama set in the 1920s. Phryne is called in to investigate when a guillotine act goes wrong at a magic show and leads to the death of the conjuror's assistant, while her rogue of a father arrives from England and interferes in her relationship with Jack Robinson. Essie Davis stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective is injured during an investigation, and in his fevered state begins to suspect his landlady is plotting to murder him. Elsewhere, in his capacity as acting detective, Crabtree finds the only witness to a murder appears to be a talking parrot. Yannick Bisson and Jonny Harris star

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective becomes embroiled in the world of telegraphy when a decomposing body is discovered, and Enid's life is put in jeopardy during the investigation. Elsewhere, Brackenreid's increasingly erratic behaviour has Julia questioning the treatment he is taking to help him stop drinking

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A young boy falls into a creek and drowns, while his mother is found slumped in the hallway. Dr Stephen Andrews discovers both ingested rat poison, and suspicion falls on the grieving husband and father. The detective soon uncovers a sordid case of extortion and forgery as part of his investigation. Crime drama, starring Mark McManus

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Newly demoted Havers joins Lynley on a double murder investigation. After a girl is killed and her camping companion stabbed, two separate lines of inquiry lead the duo into a murky world of deception, corruption and prostitution in their bid to discover the murderer's identity. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small, Timothy West and Simon Williams

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: John Heaton reveals that hit-and-run driver Paddy Morgan has disappeared and may have been killed by Ray Moore's hit-man. Mia Perry grows closer to her commanding officer during the investigation, and Gina has a romantic assignation with married lover Peter Harris which fails to live up to expectations

programme.desc: Christmas special from 1995. The Meldrews' wedding anniversary celebrations turn sour as they learn cousin Ursula has died while watching Noel Edmonds - forcing Victor to defy a witch's grim warning and set out to put her affairs in order

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor's continuing campaign against bureaucrats finds another victim - a health and safety inspector who comes to check on the compost heap at the bottom of the Meldrews' allotment. Christmas special from 1991, with guest star Eric Idle

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Domestic disharmony ensues for the Meldrews as a routine trip to the garden centre starts a trail of turmoil including a scowling snowman, a laughing policeman and a mysterious saboteur. Christmas edition of David Renwick's comedy from 1994, starring Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton

programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie and Sister Mary Cynthia team up to support a deaf mother-to-be, while another patient causes great concern when a case of morning sickness proves more serious than initially suspected. On the personal front, handyman Fred's daughter is less than delighted to hear about his forthcoming wedding. Will he be forced to choose between her and Violet?

programme.title: In the Dark
programme.desc: Five months after the Polesford case, a heavily pregnant Helen is still battling with the fallout from her infidelity. Her life is derailed by a gangland shooting with tragic consequences. Just days from giving birth, she is plunged into a murder investigation involving Manchester's criminal underbelly, forcing her to question everything she holds dear. Drama, starring MyAnna Buring and Tim McInnerny

programme.title: In the Dark
programme.desc: Helen finds herself in more danger as the investigation pulls her further into Manchester's criminal underworld. Paranoid, vulnerable but unperturbed, she tracks down the gang responsible for the shooting - before the case throws up some shocking revelations. And can Theo cheat death as the gang continues to be hunted by a mystery gunman?

programme.title: Father Brown

Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford, Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa at Molineux and Newcastle United v West Bromwich Albion at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the third Sunday of Advent, Father John Dickson, Catholic Chaplain, leads the ecumenical service from the Chapel of Royal Holloway, University of London. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the University's Chapel Choir

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Thursford in Norfolk to experience a Christmas light show and walks through the four-acre lantern extravaganza. Katie Piper visits a church in North London where their Christmas Angel hampers bring some festive cheer to families in need. Featuring favourite Christmas carols with performances from Collabro, Marisha Wallace, and opera star Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final results show opens with a routine from the Strictly professionals, and there's also a musical performance from pop superstars Little Mix. More importantly, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman also reveal which two couples will compete in the dance off, leaving the judges to decide who will not be making it through to the final

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and Will find allies who can help them in their search for Will's father, the Magisterium learn something shocking, and Mrs Coulter meets a formidable foe. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Kingsley is transferred to a special-needs school, a group of West Indian women uncover an unofficial segregation policy preventing many black children from receiving the education they deserve. Co-writer/director Steve McQueen's drama, starring Kenyah Sandy and Sharlene Whyte

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman and guests review the day's Premier League action, featuring Fulham v Liverpool at Craven Cottage, Crystal Palace v Tottenham Hotspur at Selhurst Park, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium, Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at King Power Stadium and Southampton v Sheffield United at St Mary's Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal, Birmingham City v Everton and Reading v Manchester United

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet continue their search in Bristol and Gloucestershire, and while Serhat aims for glass and metalware, Christina causes chaos trying to get her hands on possibly the largest Road Trip item ever

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival of the day is widow Eve Robinson from Kings Lynn, Norfolk, who hopes that goldsmith Richard Talman can mend her diamond wedding ring. Next, Wizard Geoff turns his attention to a vintage penny arcade game which was salvaged from a bonfire over 40 years ago. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a shop mannequin and a pair of lumberjack's tree spurs

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create dishes for unexpected guests, with impressionist Jon Culshaw joining them to make a last-minute Christmas cake, and orange and honey glazed salmon

programme.title: Nigel Slater's 12 Tastes of Christmas
programme.desc: The food writer presents the first of two programmes in which he creates a selection of festive dishes, getting into the spirit of the season with ideas for both self-indulgence and entertaining. Nigel also makes a culinary trip to Norway, during which he goes in search of a special Christmas tree

programme.title: Lucky Jim (1957)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking, unconventional junior lecturer is frustrated with his stuffy university, but still wants to curry favour with the formidable head of the history department. A family gathering and an academic ceremony provide opportunities to make an impression - but nothing goes to plan. Comedy based on Kingsley Amis's novel, starring Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas and Hugh Griffith

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates how nature has tinkered with the ageing process to different effect, and profiles evolutionary anomalies. He recounts how scientists are beginning to unravel the true nature of the narwhal's tusk and the reason for its spiral shape. The spiral is also vital to the lives of snails, allowing them to leave the sea and conquer land.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the RAF Museum at Hendon in London, antiques experts Anita Manning and Mark Stacey find some real treasures among the bags and boxes to take to auction. There's a photo album of the facility in the '30s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: All the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as the contenders prepare for tomorrow night's show. Including reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Martel Maxwell compete in the money-making challenge, scouring the antiques shops of Cheshire with the assistance of experts Roo Irvine and James Braxton. Former soldier Rav fancies some militaria, while Martel reveals a fine ability to hold a tune and indulges her musical side with the purchase of a grand piano. But which one will have shown to have played to their strengths at the auction in Bolton?

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: During the hottest time of year, the waterhole becomes busier in the cool of night. Using remote cameras, the team uncovers the new dynamics of the period following sunset. Hyena move into the area, but they are so elusive that it is hard to know how big the clan is. Their arrival has a dramatic effect on other predators in the area, and no lions or leopards are spotted while they are around. Ella Al-Shamahi discovers how the increased footfall has changed the shape of the waterhole and how, as temperatures rise, the waterhole residents' behaviour is also changing

programme.title: The Mercy (2017)
programme.desc: The story of amateur sailor Donald Crowhurst and his solo attempt to circumnavigate the globe in 1968. The struggles he confronted on the controversial voyage, while his family awaited his return, is one of the most enduring mysteries of recent times. Factual drama, starring David Thewlis and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul travels to the very north of the British Isles, to The Shetland Isles - islands which are as Scandinavian as they are British. During his northerly adventures, Paul lands on the island of Yell, where he meets with local Brydon Thomason, from Shetland Nature, and gets the amazing opportunity to see wild otters up close.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories. With Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: This winter sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin, alongside paintings by Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum next spring. This documentary charts the affinities between Munch's art and Emin's, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britain's pre-eminent artists

programme.title: The Hurt Locker (2008)
programme.desc: Two American bomb disposal experts operating in Iraq are assigned a new leader after their previous one dies in action. However, the sergeant's reckless attitude and love of battle consistently puts them at risk. Kathryn Bigelow's Oscar-winning drama, starring Jeremy Renner, Anthony Mackie, Guy Pearce and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Pegasus' commander Fisk is murdered following Roslin's pledge to stamp out the fleet's black market and introduce new trade regulations. Lee is charged with solving the crime but finds himself in danger when his investigation leads deep into military, civilian and government circles. Sci-fi drama, with Jamie Bamber and Mary McDonnell

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The team are called into action to investigate a Colonial mining operation. Meanwhile, an implacable Cylon raider sets out on a destructive rampage, carrying out a series of hit-and-run attacks on the fleet. The exhausted Viper pilots have no choice but to do battle with their formidable foe, whom they nickname Scar

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson plays clips featuring mice that invaded a farm in Australia and a swarm of locusts

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and company swim with manta rays in Indonesia, visit a town devoted to Taekwondo in South Korea, and get messy during Holi, the Hindu Festival of Colours

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield build an 'adventure den' for nine-year-old Rufus, who has leukaemia, and loves climbing around even though he cannot always join in with his friends

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve focuses on woodland wildlife, which hides some of the most fascinating and secretive animals

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve explores the urban jungle, including why pigeons are the perfect prey for peregrine falcons, and how bats see the using sound, while uncovering their favourite hideaways

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: During the hottest time of year, the waterhole becomes busier in the cool of night. Using remote cameras, the team uncovers the new dynamics of the period following sunset. Hyena move into the area, but they are so elusive that it is hard to know how big the clan is. Their arrival has a dramatic effect on other predators in the area, and no lions or leopards are spotted while they are around. Ella Al-Shamahi discovers how the increased footfall has changed the shape of the waterhole and how, as temperatures rise, the waterhole residents' behaviour is also changing

programme.title: Robot Wars: Battle of the Stars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the second of two festive specials. Paralympic gold medallist Kadeena Cox, football star Robbie Savage, history presenter Neil Oliver and Rizzle Kicks singer Jordan Stephens design their dream robot with the help of an experienced engineer, then battle one another in the arena. Who will become the Celebrity Robot Wars champion?

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson reveal the stories and legends behind some of Britain's festive traditions. Here they follow the story of Christmas on these shores from its origins, through the lavish partying of Elizabethan times, to the banning of the celebrations in the 17th-century. Along the way they discover that carols were once as much about dancing as singing, and find out why a holy site in Norfolk, known as England's Nazareth, once rivalled Jerusalem and Rome as a destination for pilgrims

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and baking enthusiast Jane Asher presents a selection of festive recipes from various BBC cookery shows, as demonstrated by some of the nation's favourite TV chefs and cooks. Among the treats highlighted are Mary Berry's Christmas cake, Monica Galetti's coconut buns, Michel Roux Jr's terrine with chicken and pistachios, Tom Kerridge's spiced orange cake, and Lorraine Pascale's mince pies with a twist. Plus, Nigel Slater prepares a stilton and pastry wonder, and the Hairy Bikers demonstrate how to make a turkey and cranberry pie using the leftovers from a traditional Christmas dinner

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King are joined by actress Sally Lindsay to create pici verde pasta with Christmas meatballs, beetroot and goats' cheese risotto, and a turkey tortilla soup

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a profile of the American actress, who died in December. Debbie became a mainstay of Hollywood's so-called 'golden age' with her appearance in Singin' in the Rain, and the programme makes use of rare archive interviews in which the star discusses her favourite film roles. Also covered is Debbie's private life, including the collapse of her marriage to Eddie Fisher, and her relationship with her daughter Carrie Fisher, who also died in December, one day before her mother

programme.title: Half a Sixpence (1967)
programme.desc: A humble draper's assistant in Edwardian London unexpectedly inherits a fortune from his grandfather. In his newly minted circumstances, he starts to drift away from his old friends in favour of the company of high-society swells, and upset his girlfriend when his head is turned by a lady from the upper classes. Musical based on HG Wells' Kipps, starring Tommy Steele, Julia Foster, Cyril Ritchard and Penelope Horner

programme.title: Astaire and Rogers Sing George and Ira Gershwin
programme.desc: A compilation of classic songs written by the brothers for musicals starring Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Stockport Town Hall in Greater Manchester, where Philip Serrell examines a collection of evocative war diaries and Mark Stacey falls for a mosaic necklace and earring set. Paul Martin takes time out to explore the history behind the town's air-raid shelters

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores unusual and unexplained natural events that occur around the world during winter, enlisting the aid of leading scientists to uncover why these strange events happen. The programme examines unusual weather conditions, including tornadoes made of snow, and animals behaving in unexpected ways, from snowboarding crows to penguins attempting death-defying feats

programme.title: A Merry Tudor Christmas with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian recreates festive traditions from the reign of Henry VIII. With the aid of food historian Annie Gray, she prepares two royal feasts, including a stuffed boar's head served by a choir, and the ancestors of the modern mince pies and Christmas cake. Lucy also gets a taste of the rabble-rousing anarchy of the Lord of Misrule, listens to carol singers performing a song written by Henry VIII himself, and watches a special royal performance involving dancing stags

programme.title: Whisky Galore! (2016)
programme.desc: In 1943, disaster strikes on the remote Hebridean island of Todday when they run out of whisky. Salvation arrives when a ship carrying 50,000 cases of whisky founders on the island's rocks, but the locals clash with authority in the shape of Home Guard commander Captain Waggett. Comedy, starring James Cosmo, Eddie Izzard and Naomi Battrick

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The vicar holds a Comforts for the Troops Fund bazaar at which Mainwaring finds himself bidding against Pike for a highly prized orange. Surprisingly, the private reveals a stubborn streak that costs the thick-skinned captain an arm and a leg. Christmas edition of the classic comedy originally shown in 1976, starring Arthur Lowe and Ian Lavender

programme.title: The Untouchables (1987)
programme.desc: Incorruptible federal agent Eliot Ness is assigned the task of fighting organised crime in Prohibition-era Chicago and bringing the notorious crime boss Al Capone to justice. Aware crooked police are undermining his investigations, he puts together his own elite squad to take on the Mob. Brian De Palma's crime thriller, with Kevin Costner, Robert De Niro, Andy Garcia and Sean Connery, who won an Oscar for his performance

programme.title: The Hill (1965)
programme.desc: Five British soldiers on charges are confined to a prison camp in Libya during the Second World War, where a sadistic sergeant subjects the inmates to cruel treatment. However, when one of the prisoners dies as a result, they seize an opportunity to bring their tormentor down. Drama, starring Sean Connery, Ian Hendry and Harry Andrews

programme.title: The Unknown Girl (2016)
programme.desc: After failing to open the door to a woman who later turns up dead, a guilt-stricken young medical doctor turns amateur detective. Belgian-French thriller, starring Adèle Haenel, Olivier Bonnaud, Jérémie Renier and Louka Minnella. In French

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter W, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Yorkshire by hot-air balloon. She meets two garden designers at Scampston Hall in Malton, and catches up with an old friend at York Gate Garden in Leeds. Plus, she learns about the Yorkshireman who became the father of aviation

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In July and August, the horticulturist deadheads the roses and stakes plants to help keep the garden looking its best during the summer months, and savours the full blooms of geraniums and lilies. She creates colourful borders using cannas, ginger and dahlias, before visiting her local beach, Braunton Burrows, to seek out native sea holly, which she hopes to grow herself

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the ancestral home of his new gordon setter puppy - Gordon Castle in Moray - and Arlene Stuart joins the grooms training to care for race horses

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Conclusion. The chef continues his celebrations in his adopted county of Cornwall, using local ingredients to prepare a Christmas banquet for his friends. These include comedian Jethro, an old acquaintance who used to be an adversary on the rugby pitch when they were younger

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson conclude their special series for Advent exploring Britain's Christmas traditions, unwrapping how the season has been celebrated across the centuries. They discover why a ban on Christmas led to rioting in the streets, and how the festivities were revived with a vengeance in Victorian times. As they trace the stories behind now-common traditions - and take part in less familiar ones such as pub carolling - the Archbishop of Canterbury explains how he celebrates Christmas, and why its meaning remains so relevant today

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates food served to British monarchs from the time of George IV to the reign of the present Queen

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford joins Si and Dave as they demonstrate festive recipes using leftovers, including Christmas pudding trifle, bubble and squeak, tartiflette, gougere, and turkey pie

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness

programme.title: The Young Victoria (2009)
programme.desc: The turbulent early years of the monarch who, shortly after being crowned, became caught in a power struggle between her mother and scheming politicians Lord Melbourne and Sir Robert Peel. Meanwhile, a romance develops with her German cousin and eventual husband, Prince Albert. Historical drama, with Emily Blunt, Rupert Friend, Paul Bettany and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Katherine Higgins are at the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire, where a 19th-century Japanese woodblock and a pristine pocket watch stimulate interest. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin learns how Portsmouth influenced the Victorian author Charles Dickens

programme.title: Golf: World Tour Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the season-ending European Tour event, held at Jemeirah Golf Estates in Dubai

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon discovers what happened to some of the most memorable businesses and entrepreneurs who have exited the famous Den doors over the past 17 series. Angela takes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and new Dragon Sara Davies on a trip down memory lane when she shows them footage of themselves from former series, and she meets the latest, and youngest Dragon, Sara Davies, who has been a die-hard fan of the show since her university days

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Everyone Romesh has ever met has told him how amazing the Scottish islands are - but nobody he knows has actually ever been there. Now, in a year when travel abroad has been all but forbidden, he is heading to the Hebrides to find out if you can really have a proper adventure so close to home, spending a week visiting the isles of Skye, Harris and Lewis. Guiding him through the journey will be Donald Macsween, or Sweeney to his friends, a local crofter from the far north of Lewis - a place that lies as far north as Greenland. During their trip, Sweeney will show Romesh the spectacular rugged beauty of the islands and what at first glance look like Caribbean sandy beaches (but turn out to be utterly freezing). Rom will try his hand at the Highlands Games and try to avoid feeling up a sheep's testicles during a day spent on Sweeney's farm. Along the way, there will be a trip to the remote island of St Kilda - four hours out across the open Atlantic - and a chaotic afternoon spent with lo

programme.title: The Godfather (1972)
programme.desc: The patriarch of the Corleone crime family is hospitalised after an attempt on his life and entrusts control of his business to his three sons - the violent and unpredictable Sonny, the weak-willed Fredo, and Michael, the youngest brother, who reluctantly takes up the reins but proves to be the most ruthless of them all. Francis Ford Coppola's gangster drama, starring Marlon Brando, Al Pacino, James Caan, John Cazale, Talia Shire, Robert Duvall and Diane Keaton

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: When four villains lay siege to a bank, Angela sends Noah and Sylar after them. As Peter tries to avert a cataclysmic future, Hiro and Ando trace Daphne to Germany - but fall foul of the Haitian. Tracy Strauss leaves Washington in search of answers about Niki, Claire asks Meredith to teach her how to fight, and the results of Suresh's experiment take him by surprise

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter must embrace a dangerous ability to prevent a cataclysmic ending to a future where almost everyone has powers. After allowing Angela's half of the formula to be stolen, Hiro and Ando set out to unmask the villain who hired Daphne, seeking help from an unlikely source. As Noah gets rid of his new partner, Claire hits the road in pursuit of an enemy from her dad's old files. Meanwhile, a romance develops between two heroes, and Linderman and Molly return

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe and Cameron are driven further apart when they end up competing for the same job - and tension rises even further when Cameron deliberately sabotages his rival's chances. Skyler, Jeong and Josh work together as a team to save a gravely ill patient, under Henrik's watchful eye. Josh begins to see there is more to Skyler than he gave her credit for. Sacha and Jodie band together after their explosive domestic outburst

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Distinguished graduates of Royal Holloway, University of London and the University of Sussex battle for a place in the semi-finals. Among those competing are ceramics designer Emma Bridgewater, TV historian Fern Riddell, BBC Parliamentary correspondent Mark D'Arcy and sci-fi writer and critic Kim Newman. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of composer Vaughan Williams' The Lark Ascending, which was written in 1914 as Europe approached the First World War. The programme culminates in a performance by 15-year-old violinist Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews at Shirehampton Public Hall, Bristol, where the piece was premiered in December 1920. Featuring contributions by actor Peter Sallis, performer Tasmin Little, and critic and friend of the composer, Michael Kennedy

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on May 10, with performances by artists including Kylie Minogue, the Wonderstuff, Mantronix featuring Wondress, New Kids on the Block, Michael Bolton, En Vogue, Thunder, Adamski and Beats International

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on May 18, with performances by artists including Hothouse Flowers, the Pasadenas, the Chimes, Sam Brown, Adamski, Depeche Mode, Eurythmics and Paul Young

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: With the advent of commercial television in the UK, ITV rivalled the BBC for airtime and lured viewers in with the new language of advertisements. However, these had not yet reached the impact they had seen in the USA, the home of the TV jingle, where a 30-second tune could make or break a brand, as seen in the competing fortunes of Coca-Cola and Pepsi. Neil reveals the lasting musical power of jingles, idents and advertisements, including the long-forgotten test card tunes sampled in American hip-hop

programme.title: Dionne Warwick at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances by the US singer, one of the most-charted female vocalists of all time

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The singer looks back on her 50-year career, which began with the release in 1962 of her first single Don't Make Me Over and has most recently seen her re-record some of her best-known hits. The entertainer also discusses the death of her cousin Whitney Houston earlier this year, fighting racism, and the importance of her Christian faith

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by prominent soul artists and groups of the decade, including Aretha Franklin, Billy Preston, the Tams, Curtis Mayfield, Bill Withers, the Stylistics, Gil Scott-Heron and the Jacksons

programme.title: Bros: After the Screaming Stops (2018)
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s Bros were one of the biggest bands in the world. Having sold out stadiums, they were the youngest to this day to play Wembley Stadium and their Push album went to Number 1 in more than 20 countries, selling 10 million copies worldwide. After three years of hysteria wherever twins Matt and Luke Goss went, this documentary recalls the aftermath - the fragility of fame and the heartbreak that followed. It also charts the pair's professional reunion 28 years on, having hardly spoken and not played together since their split, as they prepare to play London's O2

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Music compilation show celebrating some of the most popular boy bands to have graced TV screens since the 1960s. The programme highlights performances by acts defined by vocal harmonies, synchronised dance-steps and character appeal, including JLS, the Four Tops, the Monkees, Westlife, Village People and Blazin' Squad

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of composer Vaughan Williams' The Lark Ascending, which was written in 1914 as Europe approached the First World War. The programme culminates in a performance by 15-year-old violinist Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews at Shirehampton Public Hall, Bristol, where the piece was premiered in December 1920. Featuring contributions by actor Peter Sallis, performer Tasmin Little, and critic and friend of the composer, Michael Kennedy

programme.title: All Aboard: The Great Reindeer Migration
programme.desc: Documentary follows Norway's Sami reindeer herds as they migrate across the mountainous region of Finnmark, far north of the Arctic Circle. The journey, a little over 160 miles, takes the herd a week, travelling north from the inland winter feeding grounds to their coastal summer pastures. The film travels with the Sara family and their herd around the clock, as they cross the arctic wilderness to reach the coastal island of Kvaloya Fala in time for the calves to be born

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata and Hakon continue their investigation in Borgarnes, where the chief of police at the time of Tomas's disappearance admits mistakes were made. Back in Reykjavik, Arnar discovers he has missed two telephone calls from Fannar Thorsson. Crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Hakon calls from Borgarnes, furious that Kata did not tell him that she had escalated the referral about the Valhalla investigation. Unperturbed, Kata is determined to find justice for the victims. Crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Shadows at Sixty
programme.desc: From where they began as the Drifters to the backing band for Cliff Richard, and on to their huge success as the Shadows, this documentary celebrates the band's achievements across 60 years in what has been a time of constant change within social, cultural and musical landscapes. The Shadows were at the forefront of the UK beat boom generation and the first backing band to emerge as big stars in their own right and this programme features unseen archive, personal testimony, interviews with Cliff Richard, band members Hank Marvin, Bruce Welch and Brian Bennett and some of those they influenced, including Brian May, David Gilmour and Pete Townshend. Narrated by Gina McKee

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard at the BBC
programme.desc: As the Peter Pan of Pop reaches his 80th birthday, a look back through its archives at some of his most memorable performances and biggest hits. Amongst the classic songs featured are Move It, The Young Ones, Living Doll, Bachelor Boy, the Eurovision Song Contest contenders Congratulations and Power to All Our Friends, Devil Woman, Miss You Nights, Wired for Sound and Cliff's much-praised '80s duet with Van Morrison, Wherever God Shines His Light

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on May 10, with performances by artists including Kylie Minogue, the Wonderstuff, Mantronix featuring Wondress, New Kids on the Block, Michael Bolton, En Vogue, Thunder, Adamski and Beats International

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on May 18, with performances by artists including Hothouse Flowers, the Pasadenas, the Chimes, Sam Brown, Adamski, Depeche Mode, Eurythmics and Paul Young

programme.title: All Aboard: The Great Reindeer Migration
programme.desc: Documentary follows Norway's Sami reindeer herds as they migrate across the mountainous region of Finnmark, far north of the Arctic Circle. The journey, a little over 160 miles, takes the herd a week, travelling north from the inland winter feeding grounds to their coastal summer pastures. The film travels with the Sara family and their herd around the clock, as they cross the arctic wilderness to reach the coastal island of Kvaloya Fala in time for the calves to be born

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary footage of the volcanic fires of Iceland and the creatures that make their home amid this seemingly inhospitable terrain. The film was shot during the country's most dramatic eruption in years, in which a huge volcano exploded under the icecap, melting billions of tonnes of ice

programme.title: Billy Connolly: A Scot in the Arctic
programme.desc: The comic heads for the North Pole, braving sub-zero temperatures and dealing with a persistent film crew to experience life in Canada's far North West. His only other company are the inquisitive polar bears who occasionally pay him a visit. First shown in 1995

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and Maggie Aderin-Pocock consider the state of astronomy in 2020 and wonder what new, exciting discoveries await across the rest of the decade

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Thanks to NASA's Juno mission, scientists are starting to peel back the layers of Jupiter to reveal the wonders within. Professor Kaitlin Kratter reveals how extreme the planet is, with the aid of a quarry and a few rocks, giving some idea of the size of the second largest body in the solar system. And at the National Ignition facility in Northern California, Dr Marius Millot uses powerful lasers normally used for nuclear fusion for an experiment giving an idea of the pressures inside Jupiter. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Holst: The Planets with Professor Brian Cox
programme.desc: A chance to see the BBC Symphony Orchestra perform British composer Gustav Holst's The Planets at the London Barbican, in a concert from last September timed to celebrate the centenary of the premiere. But there's a special scientific twist to this concert, as before each movement, Professor Brian Cox discusses what modern science reveals about each of the planets

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: What is it like when your mum becomes your dad? Twelve-year-old Tilly’s mum came out as transgender when she was 7 years old. We follow Tilly as she shows us what her life is like now. Having just moved up to secondary school, Tilly hasn't told her new friends about her family as she is uncertain as to when will be the right time to tell them. When her birthday party comes up, Tilly has a decision to make - will she explain to her friends that Jack is trans or will she make up a story to buy herself more time? To help her decide, Tilly meets Nat and Faith, who both have trans dads and help her feel more confident about sharing her story. And when younger brother Mal experiences some mean comments at school, new friend Nat offers to give a talk to his class.

programme.desc: It’s Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won’t be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren’t fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet head to south Wales. There's a mad dash around the shops as Serhat tries to eke out his tiny budget, while Christina struggles to blow all of her money. They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

programme.title: EastEnders
