programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the quiz, inviting panellists to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actresses Gal Gadot and Tessa Thompson, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman, whose debut book Quite is all about 'love, life and eyeliner'. Derry Girls star Nicola Coughlan chats about her involvement in American period drama Bridgerton and comedian Lee Mack discusses the return of his comedy series Not Going Out. With music from McFly, who perform their current single Tonight Is the Night

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens continue their first Drag Race UK tour. It's an all-ages show in Glasgow and Alyssa Edwards is moved, while Crystal's death-defying act causes a medical emergency for a member of the audience

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens conclude their inaugural Drag Race UK tour as, on the final night, Secret Santa comes to town and it looks like she's bearing gifts for those participants who have been more naughty than nice

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jane Baxter and Theo Randall, plus special guest Richard Osman, while drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson's grandmother's recipe for creme caramel provides the inspiration for a simple but elegant dessert of caramel custard. She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren, with a budget of £450,000. Away from the property search, Alistair meets a national sled dog champion and takes the reins on a high-octane race through the woods

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway sets off on two contrasting walks. He heads for the hills above Aberdyfi, where he explores the surrounding countryside above the Dyfi estuary and enjoys a walk along the beach. After this he visits Merthyr Tydfil, and learns about its industrial heritage on a trek to two castles, during which he takes in some impressive iron furnaces and valley views

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous comedians take part in the general knowledge quiz, trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Catherine Bohart, Rachel Parris, Ivo Graham, Elis James, Milton Jones, Stephen K Amos, David O'Doherty and Rose Matafeo

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are engaged teachers Matt and Kerri from London, couple Megan and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son Tony and Oli from the West Midlands

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie, Maisie and Ranvir take to the dancefloor once again demonstrate their skills. The contestants face two dances apiece as they try to win the favour of judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood - and the viewers at home - if they are to be in with a chance of winning. Once the show is over, voting lines are opened, and fans are given a chance to have their say over who should make it to the final

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Lord Melchett challenges Edmund to a drinking contest that he cannot get out of - but his puritanical uncle and aunt Lord and Lady Whiteadder are visiting on the same night, leaving him trying to be in two places at once. Comedy, guest starring Miriam Margolyes, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford, Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa at Molineux and Newcastle United v West Bromwich Albion at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the latest fixtures, including the Thursday night game between Los Angeles Rams and New England Patriots

programme.title: Three Summers (2017)
programme.desc: Over the course of three years, two musicians meet and fall in love at a music festival in Western Australia amidst a microcosm of the country's society. Romantic comedy, written and directed by Ben Elton and starring Robert Sheehan, Rebecca Breeds and Michael Caton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford, Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa at Molineux and Newcastle United v West Bromwich Albion at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the third Sunday of Advent, Father John Dickson, Catholic Chaplain, leads the ecumenical service from the Chapel of Royal Holloway, University of London. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the University's Chapel Choir

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Thursford in Norfolk to experience a Christmas light show and walks through the four-acre lantern extravaganza. Katie Piper visits a church in North London where their Christmas Angel hampers bring some festive cheer to families in need. Featuring favourite Christmas carols with performances from Collabro, Marisha Wallace, and opera star Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads off on two contrasting walks, one down by the sea and the other up in the hills. He begins by taking a hop across the water from Tenby to Caldey Island. Well-known for its monastery, monks and lavender perfume, it's also ideal for a sunny stroll. He then takes a circular walk around Carreg Cennen, one of the most dramatic, photogenic and romantic castles in Wales

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen looks at the natural world in Wales, featuring archive clips of horse whisperers, pet crocodiles and Welsh moonshine

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler examines the symbols and ideas that represent Wales, from the red dragon and the red flag to ancient burial mounds and the iPod. He also learns about the Welsh influence in an Indian community, and meets the man who helped enlighten 'dark satanic mills' around the world

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final results show opens with a routine from the Strictly professionals, and there's also a musical performance from pop superstars Little Mix. More importantly, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman also reveal which two couples will compete in the dance off, leaving the judges to decide who will not be making it through to the final

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and Will find allies who can help them in their search for Will's father, the Magisterium learn something shocking, and Mrs Coulter meets a formidable foe. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Kingsley is transferred to a special-needs school, a group of West Indian women uncover an unofficial segregation policy preventing many black children from receiving the education they deserve. Co-writer/director Steve McQueen's drama, starring Kenyah Sandy and Sharlene Whyte

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman and guests review the day's Premier League action, featuring Fulham v Liverpool at Craven Cottage, Crystal Palace v Tottenham Hotspur at Selhurst Park, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium, Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at King Power Stadium and Southampton v Sheffield United at St Mary's Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal, Birmingham City v Everton and Reading v Manchester United

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final results show opens with a routine from the Strictly professionals, and there's also a musical performance from pop superstars Little Mix. More importantly, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman also reveal which two couples will compete in the dance off, leaving the judges to decide who will not be making it through to the final

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and Will find allies who can help them in their search for Will's father, the Magisterium learn something shocking, and Mrs Coulter meets a formidable foe. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Kingsley is transferred to a special-needs school, a group of West Indian women uncover an unofficial segregation policy preventing many black children from receiving the education they deserve. Co-writer/director Steve McQueen's drama, starring Kenyah Sandy and Sharlene Whyte

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman and guests review the day's Premier League action, featuring Fulham v Liverpool at Craven Cottage, Crystal Palace v Tottenham Hotspur at Selhurst Park, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium, Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at King Power Stadium and Southampton v Sheffield United at St Mary's Stadium

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents highlights of Dundee United v Rangers and Kilmarnock v Celtic. Plus, a round-up of the four Saturday matches

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal, Birmingham City v Everton and Reading v Manchester United

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The redevelopment of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, a semi-detached house in Bloxwich in the West Midlands and a grade II listed building in Milton Regis, Kent

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Insurers turn to undercover filming to get to the bottom of a suspicious personal injury claim, a scam to cash in on a mobile phone lost in a toilet goes down the pan and social media is the undoing of an opportunist as he boxes himself into a corner. An audacious claim for compensation ends up costing one man a lot more than he bargained for when CCTV shows what really happened, and in the USA a missing person investigation turns out to be an elaborate fake death scam to cash in on a life insurance policy

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at the best entertainment items bought by past teams, and watches some very famous theatre props fly at auction

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to south Wales with a couple who have spent three decades in military housing, and are looking for their very own country home on a budget of £430,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet head to south Wales. There's a mad dash around the shops as Serhat tries to eke out his tiny budget, while Christina struggles to blow all of her money. They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jordan Banjo present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Amy Fuller, world indoor champion sprinter Richard Kitty and netball star Pamela Cookey join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the search for Ian's attacker continues, a police investigation gets under way, while Jack has a different suspect in his sights and others try to cover their tracks. Callum continues in his own questioning - but could his answers be closer to home?

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the quiz, inviting panellists to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The morning of the wedding arrives and it is not just Denise's nerves that are on edge. Jim has diarrhoea and a constipated Nana warns the Royle bride that it's all downhill from here

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Deirdre O'Kane joins regulars Neil Delamere, Colin Murphy and Jake O'Kane as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two putting their comedic spin on questions posed by audience members joining the show via Zoom. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actresses Gal Gadot and Tessa Thompson, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman, whose debut book Quite is all about 'love, life and eyeliner'. Derry Girls star Nicola Coughlan chats about her involvement in American period drama Bridgerton and comedian Lee Mack discusses the return of his comedy series Not Going Out. With music from McFly, who perform their current single Tonight Is the Night

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens continue their first Drag Race UK tour. It's an all-ages show in Glasgow and Alyssa Edwards is moved, while Crystal's death-defying act causes a medical emergency for a member of the audience

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens conclude their inaugural Drag Race UK tour as, on the final night, Secret Santa comes to town and it looks like she's bearing gifts for those participants who have been more naughty than nice

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jane Baxter and Theo Randall, plus special guest Richard Osman, while drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson's grandmother's recipe for creme caramel provides the inspiration for a simple but elegant dessert of caramel custard. She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: An Evening with Peter Alliss
programme.desc: A celebration of the golf commentator and broadcaster's life and career, featuring anecdotes from his wife Jackie, golfers Tom Watson, Darren Clarke and Gary Player, and celebrities including Gary Lineker, Terry Wogan, Chris Evans, Bruce Forsyth, Jimmy Tarbuck, Hazel Irvine and Steve Rider. Plus, clips from some of his popular programmes, including Pro-Celebrity Golf, A Round with Alliss and A Golfer's Travels. Narrated by Sue Johnston

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp tries her hand at glass-blowing in Stourbridge

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous comedians take part in the general knowledge quiz, trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Catherine Bohart, Rachel Parris, Ivo Graham, Elis James, Milton Jones, Stephen K Amos, David O'Doherty and Rose Matafeo

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are engaged teachers Matt and Kerri from London, couple Megan and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son Tony and Oli from the West Midlands

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie, Maisie and Ranvir take to the dancefloor once again demonstrate their skills. The contestants face two dances apiece as they try to win the favour of judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood - and the viewers at home - if they are to be in with a chance of winning. Once the show is over, voting lines are opened, and fans are given a chance to have their say over who should make it to the final

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Lord Melchett challenges Edmund to a drinking contest that he cannot get out of - but his puritanical uncle and aunt Lord and Lady Whiteadder are visiting on the same night, leaving him trying to be in two places at once. Comedy, guest starring Miriam Margolyes, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford, Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa at Molineux and Newcastle United v West Bromwich Albion at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the latest fixtures, including the Thursday night game between Los Angeles Rams and New England Patriots

programme.title: Three Summers (2017)
programme.desc: Over the course of three years, two musicians meet and fall in love at a music festival in Western Australia amidst a microcosm of the country's society. Romantic comedy, written and directed by Ben Elton and starring Robert Sheehan, Rebecca Breeds and Michael Caton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the third Sunday of Advent, Father John Dickson, Catholic Chaplain, leads the ecumenical service from the Chapel of Royal Holloway, University of London. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the University's Chapel Choir

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Thursford in Norfolk to experience a Christmas light show and walks through the four-acre lantern extravaganza. Katie Piper visits a church in North London where their Christmas Angel hampers bring some festive cheer to families in need. Featuring favourite Christmas carols with performances from Collabro, Marisha Wallace, and opera star Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: On the 70th anniversary of one of her career-defining films All about Eve, Talking Movies looks back at the life and times of Bette Davis, regarded as one of the greatest actresses in Hollywood history. Davis worked for more than 60 years and made over 100 films, including classics such as Whatever Happened to Baby Jane? and Dark Victory. She was a trailblazer for the independent actress who feuded with studio bosses at a time when women in Hollywood were not expected to be assertive. The programme includes of one of the last major interviews Davis gave on a film set to Talking Movies host Tom Brook, filmed on location in Maine where she was shooting her penultimate film, The Whales of August, which co-starred silent screen icon Lillian Gish. This tribute to Davis also includes interviews with younger actresses who still regard Davis as an inspirational force and an assessment of her body of work from a range of critics, film-makers, producers and her many fans. More recently, Davis ha

programme.desc: Ross Couper and Tom Oakes perform one of their sets rooted in Scottish and Irish traditions

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Seachd Là (News Review)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, Darren MacLean sings O Thig-A-Nuas, and A Spioraid Naoimh and Mairi MacInnes perform Psalm 67, Mary Ann Maclver shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Eòrpa (European Current Affairs)
programme.desc: Annabel Maclennan investigates loneliness among young and old in the Highlands and Islands and the effect coronavirus restrictions have had on a growing problem. She also looks at the impact the University of the Highlands and Islands has had as it approaches 10 years since it was awarded university status

programme.title: Dreach Ùr Na Nollaig
programme.desc: Shona Masson helps John McDiarmid make candles from logs, old candles and broken crayons. Angela MacMillan makes a Christmas stocking by upcycling material from charity shops

programme.title: Bill W
programme.desc: Time Magazine named Bill Wilson, the founder of Alcoholics Anonymous, one of its 100 Heroes and Icons of the 20th century for the legacy he left behind in helping millions find an answer not only to alcoholism, but to many other forms of addiction that destroy lives all around the world. The film follows Wilson through his 17 years of alcoholism, the events that led to his own sobriety, the crucial years of AA's founding and growth, his battle with depression, his experimentation with LSD, and his struggle with celebrity status in an anonymous society

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh joins Julie, Phil Cunningham and Anna-Wendy Stevenson for a beautiful version of An Cailín Deas Óg

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Ingrid tries to keep Erik hidden in the basement, but when Alfred needs help to retrieve his wrecked boat, the imposter is forced to play the role of his twin brother Adam. Ingrid also has to give a presentation to investors that her husband was supposed to do, while Karin travels to Unstad to find out who her father's deceased twin really was. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju and Rebekka Nystabakk

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and a team of antiques dealers visit Middlesbrough. Tony Geering tries to catch himself some Mouseman, Corrie Jeffrey goes all out to buy a whisky decanter and Jo Brayshaw turns grime detector when presented with a dirty piece of Whitefriars

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Duncan, Tracey, Val, Frank, Freddie, Diana, Kate, Pierrette and Lawrence are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ruth, Pete, Dan and Jade pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai make a heart-breaking decision, and Marlon and Rhona talk fondly

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla's worst nightmare comes true as Peter puts his life at risk. Meanwhile, Ryan, Sally and Tim take in the scene at Number 6, and Gary finds a distressed Faye locked in Ray's office

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: The second leg of the tour takes the boys to Italy, where they're holing up at a traditional Palazzo in Florence. Assistant Manager John Barnes thinks the lads need a bit of culture, so sends them off sightseeing to take in the Ponte Vecchio and Michelangelo's David. At training, fitness coach Luke Worthington puts the boys through their paces, but it's not looking good as the team is carrying so many injuries there are only three fit players. Plus, they've lost one of their biggest characters - Neil 'Razor' Ruddock - who had to leave the tour in Paris to return to London for tests on his heart

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha and Alex Hollywood help to prepare a festive family feast, including Nadia's annual tradition of hot sausage and cheddar rolls for breakfast, ginger gammon with tartiflette for lunch, a recipe for the ultimate turkey dinner, and Alex's delicious alternative to dessert with a white chocolate, raspberry and cranberry brioche for pudding

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, a Christmas volunteer in a supermarket, thinks a burglar is entering overnight and decides to stop him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo forgets to include Mr Cat in his letter to Santa! To make amends he follows Weasel, dressed as an elf, who leads him to the AeroFizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Animation following the kindly but accident-prone Mr Magoo. Magoo mistakes Weasel for a tuna being pursued by a fisherman.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Fast flowing waters await Danny in today's dare as he takes on a white-water rafting course including the UK's steepest man-made drop.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean becomes obsessed with gaming after being introduced by Mrs. Wicket's nephew. He decides he must have his own console!

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: When the Mystery Inc. gang gets lost in the Pocono Mountains of Pennsylvania they stumble into Psychic University well off the beaten track

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme, but the excitement of a space adventure might prove too much for Robin to handle

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James starts his countdown to Christmas with a feast of festive food and fun, joined by comedians Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont, singer Katherine Jenkins and chefs Daniel Clifford and Sabrina Gidda. Plus, there's a Masterclass in Christmas cake

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare Glamorgan sausages with chilli jam, indulgent roast chicken with cranberry stuffing and 'naughty' mash, and the 'ultimate' nut roast

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. However, secrets from the past surface after the murderer strikes again, and the repercussions are felt further than they were originally intended. With Neil Dudgeon, Neil McCaul, Sharon Small, Marc Elliott and Arabella Weir

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Trolley-dashing in Rylan Clark-Neal's supermarket for the chance to win £10,000 for charity are Loose Women's Andrea McLean and Nadia Sawalha, football legends Harry Redknapp and Paul Merson, and actor friends Rita Simons and Chizzy Akudolu

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the game show, asking contestants Adrian Edmondson, Jason Manford and Jenny Powell to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ann Widdecombe, Sam Nixon and Mark Rhodes and Jimmy Carr pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined by record-breaking children's author and Britain's Got Talent judge David Walliams, comedians Micky Flanagan and Judi Love, rapper and worldwide phenomenon Megan Thee Stallion, and one of the best-selling UK solo artists of all time Robbie Williams, who will be chatting and performing in the studio

programme.title: Gary Barlow's Night at the Museum
programme.desc: The award-winning singer/songwriter performs tracks from his new album, Music Played By Humans, as well as some of his greatest hits. Gary is accompanied by an orchestra at London's Natural History Museum, and will be joined by a range of special guests, including Michael Buble, Jack Whitehall, Beverley Knight, Alfie Boe, Rick Astley, Jason Donovan, Alesha Dixon, Ronan Keating and Sebastian Yatra

programme.title: Not the Robbie Williams Christmas Show
programme.desc: In a one-off special recorded at London's Alexandra Palace Theatre, the singer performs classic hits, plus new Christmas songs. He's also joined by guests including David Walliams and Jamie Cullum

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through the quarter-finals, two families go head to head in the first semi-final of the tournament, hosted by Anne Hegerty, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is at the beach building a sandcastle. Magoo thinks they are preparing to thwart an attack by an enemy riding a dragon.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tries for his AeroFizz pilot's license. Magoo ruins everything when, convinced he's fixing his roof, he pierces a hole in the airship.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The heroes of International Rescue must deal with emergencies on two fronts when an orbiting solar energy station malfunctions, placing a passing airliner - and its precious cargo - in peril

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket demands that Bean stay in to take delivery of a new lounge chair. He is forced to dress up as Mrs. Wicket to make sure it is delivered!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is charged with keeping an eye on Irma's dog Mopsy, but Mopsy is a very wilful pooch! Who is really in charge of who?

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Dr Gillman is discovered to be a fraud.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Having just moved to the boring Metropolis suburbs, Batgirl is thrilled when she intercepts a call from Batman asking for help.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Join Vick Hope, Sam Homewood, Frankie Vu and Fred Dinenage as they ask more questions beginning with How. Things get gross as they discover how the colour blue came from the loo, Frankie's in for a surprise when Vick demonstrates just how strong hair is, and Fred's feeling arty as he challenges the team to paint a quick pic.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: From the Domesday village of Symondsbury, the expert walker follows the ancient in-land drovers route, including a climb up the iconic Colmer's Hill. Her journey takes her through the Dorset Holloways, and culminates with a climb to Golden Cap - the highest point on the south coast. Her trek ends with a meal of fish and chips in a pub with sea views across Lyme Bay

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh this week are Beverley Knight and Cliff Richard, who is toasting eight decades at the top with festive liqueurs

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Hull to transform the garden of a 90-year-old land girl and ballroom dancing devotee. Frailty has seen the twice-widowed great-grandmother lose the ability to tend her once beautiful gardens, so they have become neglected no-go areas. After Alan surprises her at a tea dance, the team transforms her gardens into spaces she can safely tend to again

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

programme.title: The Grinch (2018)
programme.desc: In the magical town of Whoville, a race of human-like creatures called Whos are thrilled about celebrating Christmas. However, a cantankerous, furry green loner named the Grinch, who lives in a cave north of Whoville, hatches a dastardly scheme to ruin the festive season. Animated Dr Seuss adaptation, with the voices of Benedict Cumberbatch, Pharrell Williams and Rashida Jones

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: Pat Gibson, current number one in the world quiz rankings, recalls the highs and lows of the game that made him a millionaire in 2004. Plus, a look at memorable moments from the games of celebrities Al Murray, Harry Hill, Paul O'Grady, Jo Brand and Nick Hancock

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story
programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story
programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Thruxton, with racing from the Mini Challenge Trophy and Round 11 of the Ginetta Juniors

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and a team of antiques dealers visit Middlesbrough. Tony Geering tries to catch himself some Mouseman, Corrie Jeffrey goes all out to buy a whisky decanter and Jo Brayshaw turns grime detector when presented with a dirty piece of Whitefriars

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Duncan, Tracey, Val, Frank, Freddie, Diana, Kate, Pierrette and Lawrence are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ruth, Pete, Dan and Jade pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai make a heart-breaking decision, and Marlon and Rhona talk fondly

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla's worst nightmare comes true as Peter puts his life at risk. Meanwhile, Ryan, Sally and Tim take in the scene at Number 6, and Gary finds a distressed Faye locked in Ray's office

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: The second leg of the tour takes the boys to Italy, where they're holing up at a traditional Palazzo in Florence. Assistant Manager John Barnes thinks the lads need a bit of culture, so sends them off sightseeing to take in the Ponte Vecchio and Michelangelo's David. At training, fitness coach Luke Worthington puts the boys through their paces, but it's not looking good as the team is carrying so many injuries there are only three fit players. Plus, they've lost one of their biggest characters - Neil 'Razor' Ruddock - who had to leave the tour in Paris to return to London for tests on his heart

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha and Alex Hollywood help to prepare a festive family feast, including Nadia's annual tradition of hot sausage and cheddar rolls for breakfast, ginger gammon with tartiflette for lunch, a recipe for the ultimate turkey dinner, and Alex's delicious alternative to dessert with a white chocolate, raspberry and cranberry brioche for pudding

programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Goodbye Mr Chips
programme.desc: Martin Clunes stars as an earnest Latin master working at the Brookfield School of the 1880s, where he meets and marries his true love Kathie and finds himself touched by politics, grief and the utter devastation of World War One. Charting more than 50 years of one man's extraordinary life, the story ends in the 1930s, as a wise, elderly and much-loved Mr Chipping lives out his last days in retirement. Frank Delaney's adaptation of James Hilton's timeless classic, which has never been out of print since first appearing as a magazine serialisation in 1934, and for which Robert Donat whipped an Oscar from under the nose of Gone with the Wind's Clark Gable in the 1939 film production. Also starring Victoria Hamilton, Conleth Hill and Patrick Malahide

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence

programme.title: The Road to Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jane Horrocks and Jessie Wallace star in a one-off drama telling the story of how the long-running soap was born. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A serial killer begins targeting members of a religious group that claims to provide guidance for lost souls. Guest starring Ian McNeice and Rachael Stirling alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Gabriel joins Ruby on an outreach programme to a tea plantation, but discovers that she has not been completely honest about her reasons for wanting to go there. Back at the clinic, Lydia tries to help a patient who she believes is a victim of domestic abuse

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby is still reeling from her discoveries at the tea plantation, and must decide whether she can forgive past mistakes. Meanwhile, Mari goes head to head with Lydia when a surrogate mother gives birth in the hospital. Medical drama about a junior doctor who starts a new life in a South Indian hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Starring Amrita Acharia, Nimmi Harasgama, Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Neil Morrissey, Darshan Jariwalla, Philip Jackson, Achint Kaur and Mandeep Dhillon

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris and Kelly organise the drinks party for the American executives. Paddy tries to talk Outhwaite into leaving the farm. An accident leaves Kelly unconscious

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris and Zoe wait at the hospital for news about Kelly, and he blames Kim for the accident. Zoe confides in Becky about all the Tate troubles

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is viciously attacked while attending a broken-down vehicle stranded on the moors. When the body of the driver is discovered nearby, Rob and Rachel are determined to solve the mystery. Their investigation leads to a chilling discovery linking the case to a religious cult near Aidensfield

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The residents of Corfu are excited at the prospect of an imminent official visit from the king of Greece, and the Durrells are keen to get themselves involved with the welcoming committee. Leslie takes over leadership of a scout troop, but finds the children under his care far more of a challenge than expected. However, his match-making scheme seems to be going far more smoothly, as his mother and Spiros grow closer. Love may also be in the air for Margo as she makes her return home to the island

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Reverend Jacob Thwaite returns home to find his wife has been murdered, but while Rachel is suspicious of his alibi it's a psychic convention in the town that seems to hold the key to the case. Meanwhile, Peggy devises a plan to sell trout to a hotel in Scarborough that lands her and David in trouble with the authorities. Trudie Goodwin guests

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will threatens Zoe and warns her to keep away from his mum. Kelly wants nothing more to do with Chris. Eric blames Will for the cellar flooding at the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy and Mandy have a row about money. Eric is suspicious when he spots Dee meeting up with Will. Zoe is shocked when Becky tells her she is falling in love with her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen thinks life with Bill is too boring. Martin hears that Don has been sent to a psychiatric hospital. Emily is suspicious to hear that Alec is behind the hospital raffle

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie agree to stop seeing each other. Mike is frustrated that Don will not stand trial. Alan suggests to Fiona that she should move in with him

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The body of Dr Stephen Black is found on an Oxford tour bus, and the detectives are led to sprawling Crevecoeur Hall, where Hathaway spent much of his childhood. As he investigates, he is drawn to Scarlett Mortmaigne, the glamorous daughter of the estate's owner, but when another corpse is discovered, Hathaway's refusal to believe the family is involved threatens to test his relationship with Lewis to breaking point. Guest starring Nathaniel Parker (The Inspector Lynley Mysteries), Juliet Aubrey (Primeval) and Stella Gonet (Holby City), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the pleasures and perils of Dartmoor National Park - the largest and wildest area of open country in the south of England. He discovers it is home to ancient woodlands, high granite tors and vast tracts of rolling moorland, as well as churches, cream teas, llamas and legends

programme.title: The Road to Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jane Horrocks and Jessie Wallace star in a one-off drama telling the story of how the long-running soap was born. Tony Warren joins producer Harry Elton and director Derek Bennett as they struggle to create the programme, faced with opposition from ITV's formidable bosses Sidney and Cecil Bernstein, while Margaret Morris begins the hunt for the show's cast. With David Dawson, Stephen Berkoff, Celia Imrie and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: In the wake of the tram crash, a dying man makes a final wish, Sally risks her life to save a friend, and Gary relives his Afghanistan nightmare. The 50th anniversary episode from December 9, 2010

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is in need of some time alone so she heads to the pub to see if she can get a room for the night, where she learns the real story of landlord Brian. When she finds out there are no rooms free for her to stay, Maggie decides to go and stay with daughter-in-law Becka, and the pair have a rare moment of bonding. Meanwhile, Peter is working late at the school when his secretary Karen reveals her feelings towards him in a very unexpected way. Later, Alex desperately tries to find the 'lottery' winnings' to pay off the lenders

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: As the drama concludes, Roxanna is terrified by Alex's behaviour the previous night, and tries desperately to get to Alex before he confronts the money lenders. Meanwhile, Jill is still feeling shaken after witnessing Peter and Karen kissing and wonders if she should tell Maggie what has happened. As everyone gathers for the village celebrations, a sudden shocking series of events are put in motion that could potentially cost the life of one beloved villager. Starring Dawn French, Mark Heap and Gwyneth Keyworth

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James
