programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head as they meet fed-up families whose homes no longer fit their requirements. After listening to their issues, Phil tries to persuade the owners to sell and upgrade on the property market, while Kirstie argues that investing, either by extending or redesigning, is the wiser move. In the first programme, Paul and Dawn, their three children, and his twin 17-year-old daughters from a previous relationship, find their current home isn't working for them any longer. While Kirstie suggests a complete overhaul, Phil is doing what he does best and finds a selection of alternative properties

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Emma and Nigel Forde and their three boys have lived in their Essex house for the past seven years, but after a bad experience with a bathroom fitting they have let the maintenance of the family home slide, which means broken kitchen doors and some rather unusual curtains. Nigel thinks it's time to wave the white flag and sell up, but Emma wants some real investment to turn around the house she loves. Kirstie Allsopp has plenty of refurbishment ideas, while Phil Spencer has already found some viable replacement homes to buy. Will the Fordes decide to stay or go?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Bradford, West Yorkshire, Michelle is tired of the abode she shares with Andrew. The bungalow, set in rolling hills, is small and was only supposed to be a temporary home - that was a decade ago. However, Andrew loves this place. Kirstie tries to convince Michelle to stay by solving her kitchen problem and adding some feminine touches to the decor. Phil, meanwhile, has found a selection of amazing properties to buy - including a bungalow that's twice the size and a barn conversion with lots of outside space

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Taxi driver Nik Christides hosts the first party in east Hampshire, where one guest causes a stir by revealing details of a romantic encounter with Boyzone singer Ronan Keating. Later, Nik introduces his diners to the sport of Cheesy Darts, which requires one guest to smear their face with cream, before having cheese-flavoured crisps thrown at them

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-four Jax Naudi hosts the second dinner party in east Hampshire, but her theme for the evening causes confusion and leads to one guest wearing an inappropriate outfit. Although Sue Hood expresses her distaste for the host's menu by remaining silent throughout the meal, the mood improves when Jax reveals the evening's entertainment - life drawing with a nude model

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Natalie Edemska-Plant hosts the third dinner party in east Hampshire, and plans to serve dishes from her childhood to give her guests an authentic Russian dining experience. She begins the evening with traditional customs such as banning shoes inside the house, and although her food is not a complete success, the night ends on a high note with some energetic dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History fanatic Kyle Curtis hosts the fourth dinner party from east Hampshire. A self-confessed novice in the kitchen, he decides to get his guests to help him with his main course - a fish fondue - and his choice of dress code for the evening proves a hit

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue Hood hosts the final night of the culinary competition in east Hampshire, hoping to impress her guests with a Caribbean-themed dinner party. The evening kicks off with the diners taking turns hitting a pinata, followed by entertainment in the form of dancers before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Sancton, East Yorkshire and The North Star Club, owned by Carolyn Van Outersterp, who is joined in the competition by her daughter Galatea, where they hope their peaceful woodland cabins win over their guests. On arrival, guests Luke and John are critical of the cleanliness, and the pricing of the rooms raise eyebrows. At dinner, the reveal of a self-catering breakfast ruffles some feathers, but it's the lack of wi-fi that divides the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Friends B&B in Blackpool, Lancashire run by Luke and John Conway, where a lack of attention to detail in the rooms leaves one critic unimpressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Lincoln Holiday Retreat, where guests Luke and John's great first impressions are muddied due to cleanliness concerns

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Chapel in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, whose owners Mark Hinchliffe and Heidi Donohoe, pride themselves on offering something a little different in their converted chapel. On arrival, the guests are wowed by the interior and artwork on show. However, Luke and John discover some grime underneath the glamour and Debbie is concerned about her traditional Chinese bed. At dinner, all the guests congratulate Mark and Heidi on their pretty property, but things soon turn ugly when talk turns to the looming payment day as it becomes clear that the B&B'ers have grievances to air

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The North Star Club is first and owner Carolyn Van Outersterp has some cleanliness issues she wants to brush up on after receiving a score of one earlier in the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks compete for the £1,000 prize in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and keen basketball player Ken Oputa is the first to host, inviting his guests to turn up in sportswear fancy dress. He hopes his Double Dribble mushrooms and Slam Dunk salmon will prove to be winning ingredients

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer Erika Sager hosts the second dinner party of the week on board her boat in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and invites her guests to dress up as pirates. Despite the unconventional setting, her rivals are all impressed by the standard of her food and hospitality

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-two Sarah Brice hosts the third dinner party in Kingston upon Thames, hoping to impress her guests with her own take on dishes including prawns, lamb and chocolate mousse. Ken Oputa tries to make an impression on the female contestants with his romantic poetry, but finds Erika Sager switches her attentions to his rival Jamie Baker

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Jamie Baker hosts the fourth party in London, and his ambitious menu of langoustine and crab ravioli, herb crusted rack of lamb, and milloux of chocolate - combined with his choice of evening entertainment - go down well, until an unexpected turn of events scuppers his plans

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired golfer David Witcher hosts the final evening in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and hopes to impress with an American-themed menu, including Wyoming slow-cooked ribs and Louisiana seafood chowder. After dinner, he entertains his guests with a bucking bronco, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Bolton-based bricklayer Paul Rimmer, who has spent his career restoring and renovating Victorian terrace houses, sets his sights on constructing a state-of-the-art home on a budget of £350,000. However, instead of using the construction materials he has spent the past 40 years mastering, he has opted to build his new home from wood. Roping in his wife Carol, daughter Abby and a selection of favoured workmates, Paul and his team begin work on the ultra-modern, five-bedroom build, and Kevin McCloud reveals how extreme weather, exhaustion, and rapidly dwindling funds place the entire project in jeopardy

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With more than 88,000 under-18s arrested in 2016 in the UK, this edition focuses on how Wiltshire police are dealing with an increase in young people challenging their authority. Are young people being given too many chances or not enough?

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on Northamptonshire police's tackling of hate crime, following PCs Lauren El Sharkawi and Nina Crehan after they investigate a call from a family in Northampton who have been subjected to racist abuse from a neighbour. The police are also experiencing a surge in homophobic and transphobic abuse and are called to deal with one incident where the victim being targeted is just 13

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Moped delivery man Hussnain, 24, is rushed to A&E after being caught up in an accident involving a police car chasing another vehicle. Seventy-six-year-old Edward is brought in due to his breathing difficulties and a serious pre-existing heart condition, while part-time wrestlers Kieran and James come in after Kieran injures his knee in a bout

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The owners of Jack's Waterfront at St Clair Shores in Michigan are keen to hear the fiery chef's advice for turning their failing seafood restaurant around. The lakeside location is a popular summer spot with fantastic views, but an uninspiring menu and a manager more skilled at socialising than organising are preventing them from achieving year-round success

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With more than 88,000 under-18s arrested in 2016 in the UK, this edition focuses on how Wiltshire police are dealing with an increase in young people challenging their authority. Are young people being given too many chances or not enough?

programme.title: The Supervet: Bionic Specials
programme.desc: The owner of a one-year-old cat born with deformed front legs hopes Professor Noel Fitzpatrick will be able to fit it with two new bionic feet. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. In Holland, Matt Tebbutt meets a Dutch pioneer who may have unlocked the secret to growing vegetables using sea water, which could change the lives of millions of people across the developing world, while Kate Quilton takes a close look at how much sugar is in cherry tomatoes, asking if the supermarkets are labelling them correctly

programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees bidders battling to win potential treasures found in Yeovil's storage containers, lock-ups and garages. Linda has to work for her profit and tensions reach boiling point as Dan pushes Nat to her limits

programme.desc: The team tries to save the police's ailing horse wagons. An officer escapes with his life after a pursuit car is rammed by a stolen 4x4, while the mounted unit keeps the peace at one of the biggest football clashes of the year

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Johnny Vegas accompanies Richard Ayoade on a brief jaunt to the United Arab Emirates and the oil-rich city of Dubai. Richard checks into a suite boasting five balconies at the Atlantis Hotel, on the world's largest man-made island. The luxury continues with a city tour by seaplane and a taste of the world's most expensive ice-cream. The pair enter the biggest shopping centre in the world, where they enjoy a camel sandwich and a camelccino, and visit the observation deck of the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes comedian Roisin Conaty to Berlin for a two-day cultural break. The duo check-in at the vibrant nhow music hotel, which boasts two in-house recording studios with instruments available on room service. The mini-breakers sign-up for a guided city tour in an old East German Trabant car, finishing at Checkpoint Charlie. They also step inside a replica Cold War apartment, and visit a beer garden in the city's most popular park

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May build their own versions of motorhomes and set off for a bank holiday weekend in the West Country to try out their creations in the real world. The Audi R8 V10 Spyder also takes on the Porsche 911 Turbo Cabriolet on the test track, and American actor Andy Garcia does a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan joins host Richard Ayoade on a 48-hour ram raid of the Big Apple. The duo's packed-to-overflow schedule sees them scale tall buildings, grab some culture in museums large and small and even attempt to rollerblade. Experiences include trips to the Metropolitan Museum of Art and the Rockefeller Centre, queuing at the hottest patisserie in Manhattan for a cronut, and enjoying a free ferry ride to the Statue of Liberty. There's even time for Richard to reach the point of meltdown

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Time-conscious traveller Richard Ayoade is joined by actor Paul Rudd for a brisk romp round the Finnish capital. To get their bearings the pair head straight to the ladies toilet in the Torni hotel, which offers the best view of the city. They then go kayaking and snack on some reindeer jerky at the city's Hakaniemi market. Richard and Paul also visit not one, but two saunas - one of which can be found in a well-known burger chain. The duo also visit the wilderness island of Seurasaari, go foraging, and finish up in a sweet shop

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Beattie Edmondson, journalist Amol Rajan, presenter Gaby Roslin and comedian Hugh Dennis take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied from across the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy actress Katherine Parkinson, dance guru Louie Spence, comedian David O'Doherty and breakfast news presenter Bill Turnbull join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. The aim of the game is to hoodwink opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies - like whether Louie makes himself cry before every performance and if David actually is seeing a psychiatrist to cure a strange addiction

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. programme.desc: The guys discover a rare 1930s coupe at Buckey's one-man ranch and the numbers are not in Mike's favour when Frank shows off his beautiful mind

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: An Evel Knievel super-fan reveals some of the legendary daredevil's most personal items

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May build their own versions of motorhomes and set off for a bank holiday weekend in the West Country to try out their creations in the real world. The Audi R8 V10 Spyder also takes on the Porsche 911 Turbo Cabriolet on the test track, and American actor Andy Garcia does a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work of a team of mechanics who build bespoke cars for the Merseyside Police. Emergency surgery is needed when a brand-new prisoner van gets on the wrong side of a rooftop protestor. Boss Mick spends some time behind bars - all in the name of research, while the mounted unit needs help to get to the Liverpool-Everton derby

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: Mick Sweetmore's team tries to fix a radio issue in a bid to keep the new pursuit cars on the tail of Merseyside's criminals. There's a surprise visit from the fleet's oldest member, and the bosses get dirty hands when things go wrong on the road

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers in Hidalgo are forced to close the bridge when it's stormed by dozens of asylum seekers trying to jump the queue of people waiting to seek refuge in the USA

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A 12-year-old boy turns up at one of the bridges on his own, claiming that his mother has been kidnapped by the cartels and he has been living on the streets

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actor Mackenzie Crook, wildlife presenter Chris Packham, writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell and comedian Rhod Gilbert join David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show. Rob Brydon chairs as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with odd personal statements that may or may not be true

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly and Dara O Briain join David Mitchell's team while Barry Cryer and Sue Perkins are captained by Lee Mack, on the panel game where contestants try to fool their opponents into mistaking fact for fiction and vice versa. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents the comedy panel show, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests Alex Jones, Chris Tarrant, Alexander Armstrong and Mel Giedroyc to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee interrupts Lucy's favourite TV programme by plonking himself down next to her on the sofa, eating her chocolates and talking loudly all the way through the dialogue. But when he suddenly can't remember the name of the leading actor and his hypochondria tells him he is losing his memory, Lucy's evening is completely ruined

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Unable to convince Lucy that he did not smash a blue and white antique vase that belonged to her grandmother, Lee summons the family to a meeting to flush out the culprit

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy takes the kids away to her parents while Lee stays in the house to oversee the building work being done their kitchen, but unfortunately the tradesman he has employed has an annoyingly relaxed attitude to deadlines. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy are strong-armed into organising a Christmas variety show to raise money for their children's school. However, things prove to be far from plain sailing, as quality acts are worryingly thin on the ground and an animal impersonator as the only confirmed performer. Who can the duo turn to - surely not Toby and uptight wife Anna? With raw egg-juggling and some knife throwing for good measure, what could possibly go wrong - especially in a live instalment of this award-winning sitcom from 2018? Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden star

programme.title: Meet The Richardsons at Christmas
programme.desc: The families attempt to create the perfect Christmas for daughter Elsie. Jon is against the commercialisation of the season and wants to keep the season as pure, but Lucy just wants a big tree

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Guests James Acaster, Dane Baptiste, Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne and Holly Walsh join host Dara O Briain and regular Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at events from July 2016 through the medium of games and discussion

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedy writer Jennifer Saunders takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from November 2018, with artist Grayson Perry and stand-up Matt Forde joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: Dave Gorman focuses on the role faith plays in modern life as he performs a series of witty stand-up shows

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy panel quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the topics of love and loveliness. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are stand-up and The Last Leg co-host Josh Widdicombe, Irish actress and comedienne Aisling Bea and author and comedian Tony Hawks

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts the fourth annual Christmas special, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Taking to the stage for tonight's festive line-up are Ahir Shah and Laura Lexx, two performers making their Live at the Apollo debuts following successful shows at this year's Edinburgh Fringe, while the bill is completed by Gary Delaney

programme.title: Dara O Briain's Go 8 Bit
programme.desc: Dara O Briain presents the very best moments from the first two series, including David James playing Tekken, and Daniel Sloss getting stitched up in FIFA

programme.title: The Indestructibles
programme.desc: Multi-terrain board sports push Tim and Gendle to their limits, including high speed slaloms and an attempt at the world's first dry ski loop the loop

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Mexican Cartels exploit an old migrant route, thick with brush, to traffic drugs across the Rio Grande. Plus, in Nogales Arizona, law enforcement works round the clock to counter traffickers' endlessly inventive techniques for smuggling drugs. Custom and Border Protection officers take apart one particularly suspicious vehicle that may be hiding drugs in a compartment that's extremely difficult to access

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Custom and Border Protection officers in the Port of Miami and on the legendary Miami River are on high alert over a resurgence of cocaine smuggling

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The seafood chef continues to explore the Far East's most diverse food cultures with a visit to Sri Lanka, where he discovers a wealth of hot and spicy vegetable curries. He then ventures on to Bali and finds food has a religious significance on the island, before sampling what he believes is the best roast pork he has ever tasted

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the offbeat event from Montreal, Canada where replicas of a DeLorean and the Ghostbusters' Ecto-1 are among the vehicles taking part in the downhill race

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May show off the classic luxury vehicles they bought for the price of a Ford Mondeo. The Nissan GT-R is given a workout around Japan's Fuji circuit, and entrepreneurs Peter Jones and Theo Paphitis are the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May buy second-hand lorries and set about discovering how difficult it is to become an HGV driver. The Porsche 911 and Lamborghini Gallardo are put to the test, and Michael Parkinson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank finally get access to their white whale pick, while in Texas, the guys meet a collector who has filled an entire town with his mammoth stockpile

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz try to acquire Star Wars goodies in California

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the offbeat event from Auckland, where teams create vehicles without engines and race downhill

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actor Mackenzie Crook, wildlife presenter Chris Packham, writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell and comedian Rhod Gilbert join David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show. Rob Brydon chairs as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with odd personal statements that may or may not be true

programme.title: Groundhog Day (1993)
programme.desc: An obnoxious TV weatherman reporting on a small town's annual festival finds himself caught in a bizarre time loop, and is forced to relive the same day over and over again. While at first he tries to exploit his predicament, he is ultimately driven to mend his ways and adopt a less cynical attitude. Comedy, starring Bill Murray, Andie MacDowell, Chris Elliott and Stephen Tobolowsky

programme.title: Eddie Eats Christmas
programme.desc: Eddie Hall heads to Bradford to challenge a 4kg naan bread that's accompanied by turkey curry, and he tries out his throwing arm against a local strongman

programme.title: Eddie Eats Christmas
programme.desc: Eddie Hall takes on one of his biggest challenges to date when he goes up against the 12 courses of Polish Christmas in the Midlands

programme.title: Big Zuu's Christmas Eats
programme.desc: AJ Tracey, JME, Julie Adenuga and Loyle Carner chat around the dinner table while Zuu serves Big Zuu Bissap, a V-Gang Gobi Christmas Curry and a next-level Christmas dinner

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Ethiopia, where anthropologist Mike Tamerat shows him a side of the country in stark contrast to the common public image of a nation devastated by famine. Romesh experiences local traditional medicine and jazz music, as well as learning more about the country's oppressive political regime that has led to decades of turmoil for a country that stands on the brink of a new era in its history

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of the most memorable news stories of 2018, with captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop, and assorted hosts and special guests from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy panel quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the topics of love and loveliness. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are stand-up and The Last Leg co-host Josh Widdicombe, Irish actress and comedienne Aisling Bea and author and comedian Tony Hawks

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the quiz that tests the most obscure parts of contestants' general knowledge, awarding points for interesting answers as well as correct ones. Regular panellist Alan Davies is joined by Bill Bailey, Jimmy Carr and Only Connect host Victoria Coren Mitchell to answer fiendish questions on the topic of jargon

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts the fourth annual Christmas special, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Taking to the stage for tonight's festive line-up are Ahir Shah and Laura Lexx, two performers making their Live at the Apollo debuts following successful shows at this year's Edinburgh Fringe, while the bill is completed by Gary Delaney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell is joined by comic actor Greg Davies and TV presenter Konnie Huq, while Lee Mack teams up with former cricketer Phil Tufnell and comedian Marcus Brigstocke, as the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon continues

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry unleashes a batch of fiendishly puzzling questions to test the general knowledge - or ignorance - of Jo Brand, Liza Tarbuck, Sue Perkins and regular panellist Alan Davies, who reveal what they know about topics including jelly, jam and juice

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Aisling Bea, Holly Walsh and Nikki Bedi join regular panellist Alan Davies, as host Sandi Toksvig finally gets round to a bit of procrastination. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Go 8 Bit: DLC
programme.desc: Dara O Briain's Go 8 Bit's companion show, hosted by Go 8 Bit's resident games expert Ellie Gibson with team captains Steve McNeil and Sam Pamphilon

programme.title: Go 8 Bit: DLC
programme.desc: Rhianna Pratchett, the writer behind the rebooted Tomb Raider series, joins Ellie Gibson, Steve McNeil and Sam Pamphilon to add another title to the Hall of Game

programme.title: The Indestructibles
programme.desc: Gendle and Tim are dropped into a downhill bike park and create a human marble run using motorbikes and riders wearing zorbs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: The United States' entry into the Second World War following the attack on Pearl Harbor, focusing on Roosevelt's decision to engage German forces as well as Japanese

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The crimes of Amelia Dyer, who promised mothers that for a fee she would adopt their babies and raise them as her own. In fact, she neglected and murdered countless infants over a 30-year period in Victorian Britain. This episode tells the story of Dyer and of Evelina Marmon, one of the desperate young mothers she deceived

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: This edition examines the efforts of the British and Canadians who fought elite German divisions to capture the city of Caen, the key to breaking out of Normandy

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A small isolated country littered with strange concrete mushrooms and a secret Japanese facility that was once at the forefront of weapons technology

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo braves the freezing temperatures of the Baltic Sea and finds peace paddling a canoe on the Lakes of Finland. On his journey from the Latvian capital Riga to Tampere in Finland, he encounters medieval knights in Tallinn, grills sausages in Helsinki and samples cloudberry liqueur in a hot tub. He later arrives in Estonia, where in the setting of a ruined 13th-century cathedral he learns how the Baltic countries demonstrated their desire for independence from the Soviet Union with a 'singing revolution'

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Liverpool Metropolitan Cathedral, where finds include a sketch by LS Lowry, a 17th-century longcase clock, a model of a local ship that was once the fastest to cross the Atlantic, and a miniature piano that opens up to reveal a couple of secrets inside. The Dean of the Cathedral asks for help in identifying a mysterious chest found in 2009, having been bricked up behind a wall, and expert Andy McDonnell tells a tale of skulduggery when he encounters work by glass-maker Elizabeth Graydon-Stannus

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris crosses Australia from south to north following the original ill-fated route of the 2,000 mile-long Adelaide to Darwin railway. After beginning his journey on a luxury modern train, he transfers to one of the line's surviving steam locomotives before hitting the Outback, where he quickly concludes that a 4x4 is a more practical means of travelling. He passes through ghost towns and explores abandoned railway relics before uncovering why most of the southern section of the line eventually had to be abandoned. At Alice Springs he hitches a ride on a freight train headed for Darwin and reaches his final destination after nearly 24 hours in the cramped cab

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn follows his family's roots by exploring Metroland, the area around the Metropolitan Railway, and in Great Malvern learns about the woman behind a decorative 1860s station

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An impressive compound commandeered and repurposed by a brutal regime and an eerie complex off the coast of Ireland that became the biggest of its kind

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Examining the work of the American forces who launched a huge offensive to blast a gap in the German lines and break through to liberate France

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Chronicling the events that brought about the demise of the Third Reich, as the Allies arrived at the gates of Berlin and Hitler boarded himself up in his bunker, where it is believed he committed suicide. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his journey in the Netherlands, where he travels from Rotterdam to Utrecht, including a visit to the world's largest flower auction in Haarlem, a trip to Delft to learn how the city came to specialise in pottery and an exploration of the government buildings in The Hague

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Royal William Yard in Plymouth, where they are given the chance to cast their eyes over a number of potentially valuable items. On the waterfront, militaria expert Graham Lay has doubts about a pair of doorknobs thought to have belonged to Admiral Horatio Nelson. The team is impressed by a chess set made from parts of the aircraft carrier HMS Ark Royal, and a psychedelic painting of a band that performed at Woodstock intrigues Clive Stewart-Lockhart. Plus, jewellery specialist John Benjamin marvels at a diamond necklace the length of a skipping rope, donated to the city of Plymouth by Lady Astor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons sell a 1958 Bedford D122, Subaru and a 1969 Vanden Plas Princess. They also meet a collector who may be willing to finally part with his 220-car collection

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. By this point, the Allies were aware of Auschwitz's purpose, and faced a difficult decision over what should be done about it. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An impressive compound commandeered and repurposed by a brutal regime and an eerie complex off the coast of Ireland that became the biggest of its kind

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Examining the work of the American forces who launched a huge offensive to blast a gap in the German lines and break through to liberate France

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: The United States' entry into the Second World War following the attack on Pearl Harbor, focusing on Roosevelt's decision to engage German forces as well as Japanese

programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: The historical documentary explores how Stalin managed to seize a large chunk of Europe in the wake of the Second World War - to the chagrin of Churchill and Roosevelt

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of London's Crossrail network, a project of staggering proportions, with 10,000 personnel working across 40 sites and on 118km of track. The focus should have been on the impressive engineering feat, though delays and budgets have become the focus of attention

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the Top of the Pops archives, featuring performances by acts including the Foundations, Procol Harum, the Bee Gees, the Rolling Stones, and Dave Dee, Dozy, Beaky, Mick and Tich

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the height of the hippie era, featuring Manfred Mann, Herman's Hermits, Julie Driscoll and Brian Auger, the Alan Price Set, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown and the Rolling Stones.

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: Selection of archive performances from the decade, including music by singer-songwriters including Carole King, James Taylor, Neil Young, Randy Newman and Don McLean

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances from the decade, featuring the Eagles, Eric Clapton and Dire Straits

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from Newcastle upon Tyne to Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, along some of the earliest railways in England. He visits the first locomotive factory in the world, opened by George Stephenson, searches for the lost pit village of Marsden in South Shields, and is entertained by a comic troupe of rapping sword-dancers in Chester-le-Street, Co Durham

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Newcastle to Melton Mowbray, stopping at historic Durham Cathedral. He also explores one of the first locomotives in Darlington, and takes a gothic Dracula-inspired tour of Whitby. Finally, he boards a steam train to travel across the North Yorkshire Moors

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes a Turkish bath in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, and explores the Victorian industrial village of Saltaire in West Yorkshire. He also meets alpacas, whose fleeces made a fortune in the age of George Bradshaw, as he journeys from Newcastle to Melton Mowbray in Leicestershire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns about a successful 19th-century recycling industry in the textile town of Batley in West Yorkshire. He also discovers how the railways boosted the county's rhubarb trade and meets the great-great-granddaughter of George Bradshaw

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his journey from Newcastle to the Leicestershire town of Melton Mowbray. Along the way he learns the secrets of Stilton cheese, discovers how trains transformed the traditional British pursuit of fox-hunting and tries to make a pork pie

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from London Bridge through Kent and around the scenic south coast to Hastings. He begins by visiting the Royal Observatory to learn how Greenwich Mean Time standardised time on the railways, goes through the world's first underwater tunnel at Rotherhithe and explores the historic dockyards at Chatham

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough explores animal oddities, looking at two different animals with curiously distinctive evolutionary quirks, linked together by a common theme. In this instalment, he examines how hedgehogs and rhinos have developed protective skins

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, which have both developed incredible adaptations to help them navigate at night

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a look at animals that rely on other species, from cuckoos planting their eggs in other birds' nests to the death's-head hawkmoth stealing honey

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a look at gorillas and vampire bats, two species that have unfairly gained unpleasant reputation within the animal kingdom

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist reflects on the remarkable abilities that some plants and animals possess, such as the electric eel that helped inspire the battery and flowers that 'talk' to bees using electrical signals

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the emperor penguin and the wood frog, both of which survive in conditions so cold they seem to defy the laws of nature. The emperor is the only penguin able to breed on Antarctic sea ice during winter, while the wood frog can survive up to 65 per cent of its body mass freezing during its hibernation

programme.desc: This compelling documentary celebrates the approaching 75th Anniversary of freedom and peace in Europe

programme.desc: Eye-witness veteran testimony from men who served under fire on the frontline, taking viewers to the heart of iconic moments in the history of warfare. programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A complex of stone buildings near Hadrian's Wall that were built to keep Northern hordes at bay. Also, a top secret military facility in the American Midwest

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Gill Buley hosts the first dinner party in Basingstoke, Hampshire, but gets off to a bad start while trying to make a choux pastry. Family man Richard Carpenter and fellow guest Sophie Branfield fail to make a good first impression on each other, and Gill comes clean about her cosmetic surgery - much to the shock of marketing manager Spencer Uren and art teacher Gabrielle Hennig

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sophie Branfield invites the diners to her multimillion-pound country house for the second party in Basingstoke, and the guests are suitably impressed by her palatial home. However, despite the advantage of being able to cook in a space-age kitchen, Sophie finds her meal does not go according to plan

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Richard Carpenter hosts the third dinner party in Basingstoke, but risks alienating Sophie and Spencer with the clutter and cats that inhabit his house - and he does not help matters by suggesting the property might be haunted. Undeterred, he remains confident his cooking will win the guests round, but makes a grave error when preparing custard for the dessert

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Art teacher Gabrielle Hennig hosts the penultimate dinner party in Basingstoke. After a day of chaos in the kitchen, her calm exterior begins to crumble, but her surprising house, eclectic clutter and eccentric hosting result in an entertaining night for the guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Basingstoke, marketing manager Spencer Uren regrets doing little preparation before the party but, despite his vegetarianism, hopes to impress with a chicken dish. Gabrielle gets upset when everyone quizzes her about food and Sophie's fussiness begins to annoy people, but a surprising revelation brings them all together before the winner is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Martin and Michelle MacClay from Wiltshire have long dreamed of having a holiday home in France to escape from their busy lives along with their 13-year-old daughter Caitlin. They have chosen the Loire Valley as their perfect location because of its landscapes, rivers, historic chateaux and wonderful food. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find a property on a budget of £73,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elsa and Brian Surridge were on the verge of buying a Spanish property when the 2008 financial crisis scared them off. A decade later they're back and Brian has promised Elsa they won't leave without bagging their dream retirement property on the Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them five very different options for their cash budget of £75,000.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Joshua and Lee's search for the perfect chateau with a music venue potential continues in western France. The heat is on for Tim and Margreeth as they host 14 guests for their first fine dining and floristry weekend in the newly updated library

programme.desc: Debra tires of having to deal with Ray's interfering mother, grumpy father and resentful brother, who constantly show up out of the blue, and a white lie has embarrassing consequences. Pilot episode of the comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra sees newlywed friends making a public display of affection, and cannot help wondering why Raymond has so much trouble telling her he loves her. Ray Romano stars

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to help a struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, where the three owners regularly come into conflict as they each try to assert their authority over the business, causing confusion for all. When one of the trio stubbornly resists the changes the Scottish chef is hoping to implement, sparks begin to fly

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun

Louise is hoping for somewhere to escape with her two-year-old daughter, while Mike is looking to make the first step in a potentially permanent move

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Luxury Christmas for Less
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant and Helen Skelton speak to former supermarket insiders and uncover what the stores know about people's tastes and shopping habits - and reveal how consumers can use that knowledge to get a brilliant Christmas at bargain prices. Retail psychologists explain the covert role of party food in getting customers to spend more than they need to, and why they are wasting money on champagne when they could buy just as good fizz for half the price

programme.title: Christmas in New York: Inside the Plaza
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the Manhattan hotel over the festive period, following staff at their busiest time of the year, and the guests willing to spend up to £30,000 a night. From arranging a taste of Christmas movie magic with themed £3,000 welcome packages to planning the hotel's star attraction of the Grand Christmas Buffet, the programme reveals how much effort goes into ensuring that 400 guests have the happiest holidays.

Meanwhile, 67-year-old Suzy is set up with Fred, a fellow divorcee who is looking for some excitement in his life, and single mother Amy meets 29-year-old Geordie Scott

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eccentric banker Tom Judge hosts the second dinner party in south-east London. He hopes his Dickensian Christmas-themed evening, complete with bell-ringing, kazoo playing, a dead eel as a table decoration and 'Mr Fizzy Wigs' punch, will win him the £1,000 prize

programme.desc: History buff John Stevens presents the third festive dinner party from south-east London, where his guests are given the chance to experience an authentic taste of wartime austerity with a 1941-style Christmas - but how will they feel about the simple rations they are forced to eat as part of the host's theme for the night?

programme.title: The Snowman
programme.desc: Raymond Briggs' festive favourite, a wordless animated fantasy telling the story of a small boy whose snowman magically comes to life and whisks him off on the journey of a lifetime to the North Pole. Howard Blake's score includes the award-winning song Walking in the Air, which was taken to number five in the charts by a young Aled Jones, but is here sung by Peter Auty

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps good friends Mike Dawson and Louise Clark search for a holiday apartment in the resort of Sitges, near Barcelona, on a budget of £170,000. Louise is hoping for somewhere to escape with her two-year-old daughter, while Mike is looking to make the first step in a potentially permanent move

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Pia and Stephen Abbott have been married for 35 years and have long dreamt of owning a property in Spain. Jasmine Harman helps the couple find a home around La Marina Alta within their £130,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elsa and Brian Surridge were on the verge of buying a Spanish property when the 2008 financial crisis scared them off. A decade later they're back and Brian has promised Elsa they won't leave without bagging their dream retirement property on the Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them five very different options for their cash budget of £75,000.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting the Isle of Wight to meet a couple who have lots of unwanted militaria in their sheds. While Henry embarks on an ambitious project to turn y tank parts into hanging baskets, Simon plans to transform old insulators into studio flat lighting

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Cumbria, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore a Morris Minor to fund fixing the owner's leaking roof, and transform some unwanted wood into furniture

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Suffolk, Henry and Simon unearth a series of hidden treasures in former agony aunt Janet's garden shed, including a vintage tram bench

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole finds an unusual three-wheeled motorbike and Simon O'Brien unearths an old projector screen that he wants to turn into a work of art

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Cockermouth, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet blacksmith Jon, who spent many years working on fairgrounds. At the valuation, independent valuer Ronnie Archer-Morgan reckons there's rich pickings to be made from railway memorabilia

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to help a struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, where the three owners regularly come into conflict as they each try to assert their authority over the business, causing confusion for all. When one of the trio stubbornly resists the changes the Scottish chef is hoping to implement, sparks begin to fly

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willy's Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. On finding an inexperienced chef serving food from both the freezer and tins, together with service that reduces one customer to tears, Gordon Ramsay orders an overnight deep-clean of the premises and designs a menu to inspire the kitchen staff

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Inside Poundland: Secrets from the Shop Floor
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following the retailer's bid to shed its bargain basement reputation and go upmarket in an effort to attract new customers. Managing director Barry visits the Walsall branch to help with the launch of Project Diamond - a multimillion-pound investment into giving stores a makeover and branching into new product lines like frozen and chilled food

programme.title: The Truth About Amazon
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Sabrina Grant offer advice on the risks of online Christmas shopping, investigating why customers should be wary of buying toys from Amazon, and sharing stories mysterious Amazon parcels that arrived full of goods the recipient had never ordered. Plus, advice on how to find hidden discounts on popular premium-brand items

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree as they restore a French chateau that has stood empty for 40 years

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking at Christmas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jamie Oliver gets creative and makes a pie that embraces whatever remains from the Christmas dinner. Melting down all the leftover bits and pieces from the cheeseboard, he creates the simplest retro fondue we can make in minutes. And smashing up the leftover mince pies and Christmas pudding, the chef rolls up a treat with his 'anything goes' strudel

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: An advent calendar competition and a guide to making a crochet garland for a fantastic alternative to tinsel. Plus, a craft maker turns forgotten textiles into decorative art. Kirstie Allsopp hosts

programme.title: Luxury Christmas for Less
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant and Helen Skelton speak to former supermarket insiders and uncover what the stores know about people's tastes and shopping habits - and reveal how consumers can use that knowledge to get a brilliant Christmas at bargain prices. Retail psychologists explain the covert role of party food in getting customers to spend more than they need to, and why they are wasting money on champagne when they could buy just as good fizz for half the price

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: Best Bits
programme.desc: The second of two highlights programmes celebrating the show's 10th anniversary features the stories of popular contestants Val and Liam. Featuring a homage to the nation's favourite squirrel, a look into the mystery that surrounds the curse of the Star Baker, and the history of the Hollywood handshake

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: In London, Nastassja makes a Noah's ark-themed cake for a namesake one year old, while Molly's creates a giant butterfly cake for a birthday surprise

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: A handsome Jewish doctor makes Grace's day when he saves her - on horseback - from being late for her insemination. Meanwhile, Karen is tempted to cheat on her jailbird husband. Guest starring Harry Connick Jr

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack lands his dream job as personal assistant to actor Kevin Bacon, and Grace is visited by Dr Leo Markups, who makes her a tempting offer. With Harry Connick Jr and Rip Torn

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will has to wait another month for an answer to the insemination issue while Grace focuses on her relationship with Leo, leading to a big argument

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is curious about Alan's mysterious love life - until he finds out his brother has been seeing his former wife Judith. He later admits to Rose that he is scared he will miss Jake if the couple patch up their differences and start again. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Berta temporarily moves into the beach house with Charlie and Alan, causing tension to arise between the two men, leading Alan to find his own apartment. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Conchata Ferrell

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Steve and David were once good friends, but are now embroiled in an ongoing dispute that started with a simple off-the-cuff remark and soon spiralled into an all-out war over the ownership of a hedge. Also featured is a man who sent some workmates round to the house of a former mayor to collect money for unpaid building work, but ended up on the wrong side of the law himself when she complained to the police about his behaviour

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Another selection of neighbourly conflicts, featuring a dispute between two retired men about a new driveway, which resulted in the pair fighting in the street in the Tayside town of Kinross. The Webbs' peace in rural Plymouth was shattered when a new family moved in a few doors away, and a row about parking erupted into a full-blown punch-up. Meanwhile, Rita Grootendorst claims to have been the victim of a land grab by her next-door neighbours, although she is the one who has been arrested for assault and criminal damage over a drainpipe

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In north London, agents Del and Dael chase nearly £2,000 owed by a printing company, but the owner denies the debt and starts getting physical. Meanwhile, Stewart and Elmor find themselves caught up in a personal dispute when they try to collect rent arrears from a barber's shop, and in Bedfordshire, Del and Brian try to recover almost £2,000 owed to a funeral director. Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost £8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect £3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost £2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly £3,500 from a businessman in west London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers Hayley and Ben can barely believe their eyes when they encounter a drunk driver in the fast lane of the M6, and dog handler Glenn is given a crash course in marine rescue when he answers an emergency distress call. Plus, a monster-truck owner tries to give police the slip and a pizza-delivery driver gets a special order from the interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A van thief gives officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit the runaround before taking a disastrous wrong turn. Dan joins a manhunt but finds dodgy diesel instead, while Jon wades into a late-night assault involving a pair of TV legends. Big Jim reports a boy racer to his mum and delivers a dressing down to a driver with a taste for rude gestures

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Jon is forced to wrestle with a drunk man on the high street in Lincoln and Dan has an early morning encounter with a driver who is over the limit. Elsewhere, a wine-loving shoplifter gets his just desserts, Mark pulls over a juggernaut in jeopardy on the A1, and the helicopter is scrambled to chase down a car thief

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: People featured in previous episodes, including an Essex landlady who wondered why her tenant had stopped paying rent - only to discover that the woman was in prison for GBH and that her ex-boyfriend had moved into the flat. Plus, a music entrepreneur who took his tenant to court after receiving no rent for seven months

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A quiet Suffolk street is torn apart by a 71-year-old man believed to be deliberately breaching the peace, while the Fergusons in Wolverhampton experience trouble with their neighbour, whose extension begins to invade their space. Plus, former soldier Philip Taylor from Whitby, North Yorkshire, takes the law into his own hands after his neighbours purchase a strip of grassland that he had tended for many years and deny him access to his caravan

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Teorin and Collette had high hopes when they let out their five-bedroom family home, but it wasn't long before their tenant stopped paying rent - and then turned the house into a hostel for holiday-makers. Quickly running out of options, the couple turn to eviction specialist Chris Sharpe for help. Plus, Harrow council officers discover 40 people crammed into a potential deathtrap of a house, and a woman who relies on her rent for medical expenses tries to claw back some from her tenant

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An east London landlord who trusted his property to a letting agency discovers the house is being advertised on a Romanian website - and strangers have moved in. Plus, west London housing officers investigate a tip-off about people living in dangerous conditions behind a row of shops

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Kent couple Rachel and Chris are pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant breaks down and she stops paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Elsewhere, a tenant uses scare tactics to keep his landlord off his back, and in Harrow, council housing officers investigate a residential property that has been turned into a brothel

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Chicago PD

Intelligence investigates a string of such incidents and racing against a ticking clock, enlists Firehouse 51 to help examine the devices and prevent a further attack

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Curtis and Briscoe question a disabled boy's bereaved family in an attempt to establish whether his death was a mercy killing or cold-blooded murder, and discover his mother is not as respectable as she makes herself out to be. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A female cop is found stabbed to death in a park alongside a Hispanic male with a near-fatal gunshot wound. Further investigations lead Briscoe and Curtis to a girl who suffered a tough childhood in the care of an abusive father and drug-addicted mother, and her alibi appears to be flimsy at best. Robert Vaughn guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A newly appointed judge is knocked over and killed while trying to escape from the tabloid journalist who has been harassing her. The team is all set to press charges against the reporter, but it soon becomes clear there is more to the case than meets the eye. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a social worker, exposing a racket involving foster children. The resulting trial sees Paul Robinette place the system in the dock as he defends a black woman accused of kidnapping her baby from his adoptive parents. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman murdered while out jogging is found to be the spouse of a comedy club owner previously acquitted of killing his first wife. In order to secure a conviction, the team is forced to trace the part of a Colombian coin used in a subway. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck ad executive becomes a murder suspect when his wife and son are killed during a night he claims to have spent drinking. Ellen Pompeo (Grey's Anatomy) guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. However, when her boss orders her to back away, she turns to the NCIS team for help. Meanwhile, McGee talks to DiNozzo about whether he should move forward with his relationship. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: The team investigates a fatal accident at a naval base, only to discover a deadly virus has been unleashed which threatens the whole city

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: LaSalle's former partner is the prime suspect when a link is discovered between the New Orleans police department and an international drugs ring

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A teenage girl claims she was assaulted by a man she met over the internet, leading the police to launch a massive sting operation to bring the culprit to justice - but the decision to place Munch in the role of the online youngster has higher authorities up in arms. Hard-hitting crime drama, starring Richard Belzer, Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is raped at knife-point on a busy subway train in full view of the other passengers, and it soon emerges that the attacker is a serial offender bringing terror to the underground. A further assault later appears to occur, and a fellow passenger intervenes - but the suspected rapist claims to have merely fallen on top of the woman when the train jerked to a halt

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A famous news reporter is raped by two men but fails to prove their guilt at the trial, and decides to challenge the cops' competence with a TV debate, only to be killed by a bomb blast. With one of her previous attackers cleared of the murder, it's up to the police to find his accomplice - and it becomes apparent the journalist is not the only victim in the puzzling case

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A college student is found dead having fallen down some stairs following a struggle, and it soon emerges that a campus basketball player had previously raped her. Acclaimed drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Jessica impersonates an old friend to track down a hit-and-run driver and uncovers a sordid tale of blackmail, theft and murder. Drama starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: While staying in an opulent hotel, courtesy of Grady's fraternity brother Gary, Jessica becomes embroiled in a fight to clear Grady of a woman's murder, which is tied to Gary's past

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The sheriff is landed with the job of entertaining his sister when she leaves her husband. However, the situation is complicated when his brother-in-law is murdered, leaving Jessica with a conundrum to solve. Starring Angela Lansbury and Henry Gibson

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica helps reformed jewel thief Dennis Stanton investigate a fire that has burned the hands of a famous concert pianist, uncovering a tangled tale of arson, fraud and unrequited love. Starring Angela Lansbury and Ricardo Montalban

programme.desc: Jessica persuades Seth to visit his estranged brother, who promptly turns up dead. The sleuth must uncover whether he was killed by a curse supposedly placed on a stolen Indian pendant, which a diplomat is determined to see returned to its own country. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.desc: Benson and Stabler try to secure a conviction against a piano teacher accused of molesting his students _ but the prime witness refuses to testify, afraid that a video of his ordeal will emerge. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Rip Torn, Gary Kroeger and Jamie Rose

programme.title: Columbo: Sex and the Married Detective (1989)
programme.desc: A sex therapist tries to pin the blame for the murder of her unfaithful lover on her assistant, but the dishevelled detective is not convinced by the ruse and seeks to identify a mysterious woman in black who was spotted leaving the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Lindsay Crouse, Peter Jurasik and Julia Montgomery

programme.title: Columbo: Strange Bedfellows (1995)
programme.desc: A horse trainer outraged by his brother's gambling debts murders both him and the bookie he owes money to, then claims one killed the other. Columbo is not convinced by his story and goes in search of the truth, and finds help from an unlikely source when a Mob enforcer is sent to investigate the bookie's death. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Rod Steiger and George Wendt

programme.title: Columbo Undercover (1994)
programme.desc: The detective dons various disguises to investigate the deaths of two men who apparently killed each other, his only clue being a fragment of a black-and-white photograph, and comes to suspect the double murder has a connection to a $4million bank robbery. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Ed Begley Jr, Burt Young and Tyne Daly

programme.title: Columbo Goes to the Guillotine (1989)
programme.desc: The dishevelled detective investigates the suspicious death of a stage magician. All the evidence points toward suicide - but the sleuth is soon on the trail of an ex-convict who once conspired with the illusionist to steal military secrets. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Anthony Andrews and Karen Austin

programme.title: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star (1991)
programme.desc: The crafty detective pits his wits against a brilliant defence lawyer who has never lost a case. The attorney has murdered his rock star girlfriend to prevent her exposing his unethical courtroom tactics, and then framed her lover for the crime. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Little Richard, Dabney Coleman, Shera Danese, Cheryl Paris and Julian Stone

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead in her apartment, her son, recently paroled after serving 15 years for murder, becomes the prime suspect. Goren and Eames meet the detective who investigated the earlier case and discover the man may have been wrongly convicted - revealing a complex cover-up that implicates the policeman himself. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A fire at the side of the road prompts Stabler to pull over and assist the emergency services. He is horrified to discover the murder of a man who was set on fire - and his daughter Maureen sees everything. Christopher Meloni and Erin Broderick star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the beating and rape of an Afghan woman in Central Park. As it probes the violent and disturbing case, the victim's family turn their backs on her. Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman who claims she has been raped turns to the Unit for help, but the team cannot get enough evidence against the prime suspect and is forced to drop the case. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek
